Meta.title.context: Browser-tab / PWA title. “Numbers” stays verbatim. “CFCC” is the ENGLISH abbreviation only — Chinese uses the church's Chinese name, 中华归主海沃教会 / 中華歸主海沃教會 (see GLOSSARY).

Meta.description.source: Snap church receipts now, submit reimbursement claims later.
Meta.description.context: Meta description shown in link previews and the PWA install sheet.

Meta.appName.context: iOS home-screen name. Always “Numbers” — never translated.

Common.status.draft.context: Status pill: claim not yet generated, still editable. Pairs with 'Generated'. One word.

Common.status.generated.context: Claim status chip: the packet is built and the claim is frozen, ready to print or send for approval. Short; shown on lists and the review screen.

Common.status.needsReview.context: Status pill on the claims list for drafts awaiting verification.

Common.status.processed.context: Lowercase on purpose — appended mid-sentence to the receipt card's date · size line. Review.revertConfirm quotes this wording (test-enforced).

Common.status.submitted.context: Claim status chip: sent to an approver, waiting for their signature.

Common.status.approved.context: Claim status chip: the approver signed off; payment still pending.

Common.status.rejected.context: Claim status chip: the approver declined and sent it back.

Common.status.paid.context: Claim status chip: the treasurer recorded payment. Terminal state.

Common.cancel.context: Secondary dialog button: close without saving. One word.

Common.back.context: Generic Back button in multi-step dialogs (returns to the previous step).

Common.save.context: Primary button that commits a form or dialog. One word.

Common.saving.context: Save button label while the request is in flight. Keep the trailing ellipsis.

Common.loading.context: Placeholder text shown while a page's data loads.

Common.pdfReady.context: Toast/button text when a built PDF is waiting for a tap to open the iOS share sheet; pairs with pdfReadyAction.

Common.pdfReadyAction.context: Button label opening the OS share sheet for the ready PDF (Save to Files / Print / AirDrop). Short.

Common.language.context: Label for the language selectors (NavBar switcher aria + profile field).

Common.date.today.context: Standalone relative date label shown in place of a formatted date when something happened today (claim/receipt lists, approval & finance rows). One word.

Common.date.yesterday.context: Standalone relative date label shown in place of a formatted date when something happened the previous calendar day. One word.

Common.role.member.context: Church role shown in pickers/chips: ordinary member with no special powers.

Common.role.approver.context: Church role: a member authorized to approve reimbursement claims.

Common.role.secretary.context: Church role tag (one word): the board's secretary — an executive officer with approver authority who can also change members' roles. Church register (文书/文書), not an office assistant.

Common.role.chairman.context: Church role tag (one word): chairman of the church board — an executive officer with approver authority who can also change members' roles.

Common.role.treasurer.context: Church role: handles payments (writes the checks).

Common.role.admin.context: Church role: the administrator who anchors electronic signing.

Common.close.context: Button label closing a dialog whose action already completed (replaces Cancel). One word.

NavBar.shoebox.context: Top-navigation link. Auto-copied from Shoebox.title — translate that key.

NavBar.claims.context: Top-navigation link. Auto-copied from Claims.title — translate that key.

NavBar.profile.context: Top-navigation link. Auto-copied from Profile.title — translate that key.

NavBar.recentActivity.context: Account-menu item linking to the home page's recent-activity list. Short.

NavBar.signOut.context: Nav button that ends the session. Keep short.

NavBar.signedInAs.context: Tooltip on the sign-out button; {name} is the user's display name.

NavBar.approvals.context: Top-nav link to the approver's inbox. Keep short — phone-width nav.

NavBar.finance.context: Top-nav link to the treasurer's payment queue. Keep short — phone-width nav.

NavBar.vouch.context: Top-nav link to the vouching page, shown to attested members. One word, verb — keep short (phone-width nav). Glossary: vouch.

NavBar.account.context: aria-label for the top-right account menu button (avatar). Holds profile, language, admin, sign out. Keep short.

NavBar.pendingWork.context: Screen-reader-only text on the red dot badge marking a nav tab (Approvals/Finance) that has items waiting. Announced after the tab name.

NavBar.search.context: Nav tab to /search. Same word as Search.title (declared same-value).

NavBar.setupSigning.context: Account-menu row: the persistent door to e-sign setup. Same-value group with Identity.enrollButton.

NavBar.showYourCode.context: Account-menu row while enrollment awaits the in-person confirmation; deep-links to the QR.

NavBar.chipNone.context: Tiny status chip on the account-menu setup row. Same-value group with Identity.chipNone.

NavBar.chipPending.context: Tiny status chip on the account-menu setup row while awaiting in-person confirmation. Same-value group with Identity.chipPending.

Home.profileNudge.source: Your <link>profile</link> needs a name and mailing address — both are printed on the reimbursement form.
Home.profileNudge.context: Warning banner on the home page; <link> wraps the word that opens the profile page.

Home.profileNudgeShort.context: Compact one-line version of Home.profileNudge shown as a tappable chip on short/limited-height viewports; the whole chip links to the profile page. Keep it very short (fits one line beside a ⚠ icon). Imperative.

Home.esignNudgeTitle.context: Title of the home-page e-sign invitation card for members. Short, friendly.

Home.esignNudgeNull.source: Sign and submit claims from your phone. You'll sign in with Google again, set up in about two minutes, and then two members (or one approver) confirm it's really you in person — usually at the next service.
Home.esignNudgeNull.context: Body of the home invitation card. The two-part expectation (2-minute self-serve setup NOW vs in-person confirmation at the NEXT service) is the load-bearing content — keep both parts distinct.

Home.esignNudgeNullCta.context: Primary button on the home invitation card. Must read identically to Identity.enrollButton (same-value group).

Home.esignNudgeDecline.context: Labeled decline button beside the setup CTA — a legitimate, respected choice (never a bare ✕). Sets a terminal do-not-ask-again mark.

Home.esignNudgePendingTitle.context: Title of the home card when the member has enrolled but awaits in-person confirmation.

Home.esignNudgePending.source: Show your QR code to any two members (or one approver) at church and you're confirmed. You may be asked to sign in with Google first.
Home.esignNudgePending.context: Body of the pending-state home card. 'confirmed' = the in-person vouch (candidate-facing register: 当面确认 family, never 担保).

Home.esignNudgePendingCta.context: Button on the pending home card; deep-links to the QR on the profile page. Same-value group with NavBar.showYourCode.

Home.esignNudgePaperRepeat.source: The next one can be signed from your phone — set up once.
Home.esignNudgePaperRepeat.context: Body of the once-ever re-invitation card (its title is esignNudgePaperRepeatTitle, “Finishing on paper?”).

Home.esignNudgeCollapsed.context: One-line collapsed version of the invitation card (after it decayed). A quiet link, not a pitch. Same-value group with Identity.enrollButton.

Home.esignNudgeCollapsedPending.source: Show your QR code at church to finish signing setup
Home.esignNudgeCollapsedPending.context: One-line collapsed pending-state link on the home page.

Home.esignDutyTitle.context: Title of the amber duty card for Position holders who haven't set up signing.

Home.esignDutyNull.source: Set up signing so members can send you claims to approve. Setup is self-service; confirmation happens in person; then an officer switches on your approver duty.
Home.esignDutyNull.context: Body of the duty card, capability-forward (what setup ENABLES, never 'members can't…' blame). States the honest three-step chain; the recovery warning is the separate esignDutyRecovery paragraph.

Home.esignDutyPendingTitle.context: Title of the duty card in the pending state.

Home.esignDutyPending.source: Any officer can confirm you — a single scan of your QR code. After that an officer switches on your approver duty and members can send you claims.
Home.esignDutyPending.context: Body of the pending duty card. 'officer' = executive officer (执行同工/執行同工 per GLOSSARY).

Home.esignDutySnooze.context: Snooze button on the duty card (deliberately no day-of-week — congregations gather on different days).

Home.esignClosureTitle.context: Title of the one-time closure card shown right after the member becomes able to sign.

Home.esignClosure.source: Your claim from {date} ({amount}) can now be e-signed.
Home.esignClosure.context: Closure card body pointing at the claim that was parked at Ready-to-submit. {date} is a formatted month+day; {amount} a money string.

Home.esignClosureNoClaim.source: Your next claim can be e-signed right from your phone.
Home.esignClosureNoClaim.context: Closure card body when no parked claim exists at render time.

Home.esignClosureVouchLine.source: You can also confirm others at church — that's your Vouch tab.
Home.esignClosureVouchLine.context: Second line of the closure card: recruits the new signer as vouching capacity. QUOTES the Vouch nav tab's label verbatim (QUOTED_IN); <b> bolds the tab name.

Home.esignClosureCta.context: Closure card primary button; opens the parked claim.

Home.esignClosureGotIt.context: Closure card dismiss button; both buttons mark the one-shot card as seen.

Home.esignNudgeResolved.context: Screen-reader-only announcement when a setup card removes itself (state changed elsewhere / dismissed). Never shown visually.

Home.esignDutyRecovery.source: Don't skip the recovery step — your approver duty can't be switched on without it.
Home.esignDutyRecovery.context: Second paragraph of the duty card: the one hard warning, separated so the main body stays capability-toned.

Home.esignNudgePaperRepeatTitle.context: Title of the once-ever re-invitation card shown after a claim sat printed-but-unsubmitted for two weeks.

SignIn.tagline.source: CFCC expense reimbursements — snap receipts now, file the claim later.
SignIn.tagline.context: One-liner under the app name on the sign-in card. English uses the CFCC abbreviation; Chinese uses the church's full Chinese name.

SignIn.failed.context: Generic sign-in failure; also the fallback when the server gives no better message.

SignIn.popupBlocked.source: Your browser blocked the sign-in popup. If you opened this from inside another app, tap the menu and choose “Open in Safari” (or Chrome), then try again.
SignIn.popupBlocked.context: Error when the browser blocks the Google sign-in popup; instructs escaping an in-app browser.

SignIn.embeddedTitle.context: Heading of the warning box shown inside in-app browsers (Messenger, Instagram…).

SignIn.embeddedBody.source: Google sign-in may not work in in-app browsers. If it doesn't, open this page in Safari or Chrome — tap the menu (⋯ or the share icon) and choose “Open in Safari”.
SignIn.embeddedBody.context: Body of that warning; ⋯ is the browser's menu glyph — keep it.

SignIn.googleSignIn.context: Primary sign-in button (Google logo precedes the text).

SignIn.googleTryAnyway.context: Same button while inside an in-app browser, where sign-in will likely fail.

SignIn.notConfigured.source: No sign-in method is configured. Set FIREBASE_API_KEY, FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN and FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID in the environment.
SignIn.notConfigured.context: Operator-facing notice; <code> wraps environment-variable names — never translate them.

SignIn.devLogin.context: Heading of the passwordless login form that only exists in test mode.

SignIn.devEmailPlaceholder.context: Example email placeholder; usually kept as-is.

SignIn.devNamePlaceholder.context: Placeholder of the dev-login name input.

SignIn.devSignIn.context: Submit button of the dev-login form; '(dev)' marks it as test-only.

Claims.title.context: Page title of the claims list. Canonical — the NavBar link auto-copies this value.

Claims.subtitle.source: Draft claims need review; claims marked “Ready to submit” can be printed or sent for approval.
Claims.subtitle.context: One-liner under the title explaining draft vs generated.

Claims.emptyTitle.context: Empty-state heading when the user has no claims yet.

Claims.emptyBody.source: Select receipts on the <link>Receipts</link> page and hit “New Claim”.
Claims.emptyBody.context: Empty-state body; <link> opens the Shoebox. Quotes the New Claim button — its exact current translation is provided as mustContain and must appear verbatim (test-enforced).

Claims.counts.source: {items, plural, one {# item} other {# items}} · {receipts, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}}
Claims.counts.context: Claims-list card meta line: item and receipt counts. EN pluralizes both; zh renders bare counts with measure words.

Claims.searchPill.context: Tappable pill at the top of the Claims page that opens search pre-filtered to claims. Placeholder-style, ends with ellipsis.

Claims.subtitlePaper.source: Draft claims need review; claims marked “Ready to submit” can be printed.
Claims.subtitlePaper.context: Claims-list subtitle for users without e-sign (outside the rollout, or identity revoked). Mirrors Claims.subtitle but promises print only.

Claims.subtitleSetup.source: Draft claims need review; claims marked “Ready to submit” can be printed — or e-signed once you <link>set up signing</link>.
Claims.subtitleSetup.context: Claims-list subtitle for eligible users who haven't started e-sign setup. <link> → the profile signing card.

Claims.subtitlePending.source: Draft claims need review; claims marked “Ready to submit” can be printed — or e-signed after you're <link>confirmed in person</link> at church.
Claims.subtitlePending.context: Claims-list subtitle while the user's enrollment awaits the in-person vouch. <link> → the QR on the profile signing card.

Profile.title.context: Page title. Canonical — the NavBar link auto-copies this value.

Profile.subtitle.source: Printed onto the “Name” and “Address” lines of the reimbursement form.
Profile.subtitle.context: Explains the fields are printed onto the official (English) PDF form; the quoted words are that form's lines.

Profile.fullName.context: Field label for the name printed on the reimbursement check.

Profile.fullNamePlaceholder.context: Example person name — use a natural name for the locale.

Profile.mailingAddress.context: Field label for the check-mailing address.

Profile.mailingAddressPlaceholder.context: Example US postal address; typically kept as-is.

Profile.printedOnForm.context: Short hint under the empty Name / Mailing address fields: these values print on the official reimbursement form.

Profile.saved.context: Inline confirmation next to Save; keep the ✓.

Profile.loadFailed.context: Error when the profile fetch fails.

Profile.saveFailed.context: Fallback error when saving fails without a server message.

Profile.dutiesTitle.context: Card heading on the profile page listing the member's duty on/off switches (approve / record payments / administer). Short.

Profile.dutiesNote.source: Step back from a duty for a while — while it's off, other members won't see you as an option.
Profile.dutiesNote.context: Subtitle under the “My duties” card heading, explaining what switching a duty off does.

Profile.dutyApprovalsTitle.context: Row title for the approver duty toggle on the profile page. Noun phrase — the activity of approving claims.

Profile.dutyApprovalsOn.source: Members can pick you as the approver when they submit a claim.
Profile.dutyApprovalsOn.context: Body text of the approver duty row while the duty is ON.

Profile.dutyApprovalsOff.source: You're not taking new approval requests. Claims already assigned to you still wait for your decision.
Profile.dutyApprovalsOff.context: Body text of the approver duty row while the duty is OFF — claims already assigned remain the member's to decide.

Profile.dutyFinanceTitle.context: Row title for the treasurer duty toggle on the profile page. The duty is marking approved claims as paid.

Profile.dutyFinanceOn.source: You can see the finance queue and mark approved claims as paid.
Profile.dutyFinanceOn.context: Body text of the treasurer duty row while ON. “finance queue” = the Finance page's list of approved claims awaiting payment.

Profile.dutyFinanceOff.source: The finance queue is hidden and you can't mark claims as paid.
Profile.dutyFinanceOff.context: Body text of the treasurer duty row while OFF.

Profile.dutyAdminTitle.context: Row title for the administrator duty toggle on the profile page. One word if possible.

Profile.dutyAdminOn.source: You can change members' roles, and admins can also manage settings and electronic signing.
Profile.dutyAdminOn.context: Body text of the admin duty row while ON.

Profile.dutyAdminOff.source: Role controls and admin pages are hidden until you turn this back on.
Profile.dutyAdminOff.context: Body text of the admin duty row while OFF — the member can always return here to re-enable.

Profile.dutyTurnOn.context: Button that re-enables a paused duty — same wording as the e-sign master switch's turn-on button (auto-copied from Identity.turnOn).

Profile.dutyTurnOff.context: Button that pauses a duty — same wording as the e-sign master switch's turn-off button (auto-copied from Identity.turnOff).

Profile.teamsTitle.context: Heading of the profile card listing the member's teams. Short.

Profile.teamsBody.source: Team membership lets you view receipts filed under the team’s budget categories. Find them under <link>Search → My teams</link>.
Profile.teamsBody.context: Body under the team list: what membership grants; <link> wraps the pointer to the Search page's My-teams scope.

ReceiptGrid.pdfChip.context: Tiny chip on a card when a PDF thumbnail can't render; 'PDF' acronym stays.

ReceiptGrid.pdfThumbAlt.context: Alt text (screen readers) of a PDF receipt's thumbnail image.

ReceiptGrid.deleteReceipt.context: aria-label of the ✕ delete button on a receipt card; {name} is the uploaded file name.

ReceiptGrid.selectReceipt.context: aria-label of the selection checkbox on a receipt card; {name} = the receipt's file name. 'Select' = mark for inclusion in a claim.

ReceiptGrid.viewLarger.context: aria-label of the expand button on a card; {name} is the file name.

ReceiptGrid.viewLargerTitle.context: Hover tooltip of that expand button. Keep short.

ReceiptGrid.notePlaceholder.context: Placeholder of the free-text description input on every receipt card.

ReceiptGrid.noteAria.context: aria-label of that description input; {name} is the file name.

ReceiptGrid.caption.context: Tiny grey caption under each receipt tile: upload-date label (may render as 'Today') then the file name (data, untranslated). Keep the ' · ' separator and both arguments verbatim.

ReceiptGrid.processedSuffix.context: Lowercase suffix appended mid-sentence to a receipt card's date·size line when the receipt sits on a frozen claim. Leading ' · ' separator kept. Matches the 'processed' status word.

ReceiptGrid.claimChip.context: Chip on a card linking to a GENERATED claim it belongs to; drafts use Common.status.draft instead. One word.

ReceiptGrid.annotationPending.context: Status line on a receipt card while the background AI hasn't read (transcribed) the receipt yet. 'read' as in the AI reading the photo, same verb as Shoebox.readProgress. Short.

ReceiptGrid.annotationFailed.context: Status line on a receipt card after the background AI gave up reading it; the user will type the details during claim review instead. Short.

ReceiptGrid.annotationReadyTitle.source: Read automatically — you'll verify the details during claim review
ReceiptGrid.annotationReadyTitle.context: Tooltip (title attribute) on a receipt card's green '✓ <merchant> · <amount>' line, explaining the AI pre-read the receipt and a human still verifies before filing.

PdfPreview.chip.context: Fallback chip when an inline PDF preview fails; 'PDF' stays.

PdfPreview.rendering.context: Auto-copied from Viewer.rendering — translate that key.

PdfPreview.pageAlt.context: Alt text of each preview page image; {page} is the page number.

PdfPreview.omitted.source: +{omitted, plural, one {# more page not shown — open the PDF receipt to view it.} other {# more pages not shown — open the PDF receipt to view them.}}
PdfPreview.omitted.context: Truncation note under a long PDF's preview (ICU).

PdfPreview.open.context: Link under the preview to the original PDF; keep the ↗.

Shoebox.title.context: Title of the receipt-capture page ('Receipts'). Internally still called Shoebox (component, keys, testids). Canonical — the NavBar link auto-copies this value. NOTE: also the plain word for receipts, so location phrases quote it (“收据”页 / 「收據」頁).

Shoebox.intro.source: Add receipts as you go — snap a photo or upload a file. Select some when you're ready to file a claim.

Shoebox.upload.context: Primary upload button (opens the file picker; a separate 📷 Take photo button handles the camera). Canonical — the add-receipts dialog copies it.

Shoebox.takePhoto.context: Phone-only camera button beside Upload; opens the camera directly. Keep the 📷.

Shoebox.restoredPhoto.source: Recovered from your last visit — this photo hadn't been saved yet.
Shoebox.restoredPhoto.context: Amber note in the photo prepare dialog when a photo was recovered from an interrupted previous visit (saved locally, not yet uploaded).

Shoebox.uploading.context: Upload button while a file is in flight; keep the ellipsis. Canonical for the add-receipts dialog.

Shoebox.uploadFailed.context: Error when an upload fails. Canonical for the add-receipts dialog.

Shoebox.dropTitle.context: Heading of the full-screen drag-and-drop overlay.

Shoebox.dropBody.context: Subline of the drop overlay: accepted file kinds.

Shoebox.noteSaveFailed.context: Error when saving a card's description fails.

Shoebox.deleteConfirm.context: Message in the in-app dialog asking to confirm deleting a receipt; {name} = the receipt's file name.

Shoebox.deleteConfirmButton.context: One-word destructive button in the receipt delete-confirmation dialog, paired with Common.cancel; a verb, not a status.

Shoebox.deleteFailed.context: Error when deleting a receipt fails.

Shoebox.selectedCount.source: {count, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}} selected
Shoebox.selectedCount.context: Sticky-bar label while receipts are selected (ICU count).

Shoebox.selectedCountShort.context: Short variant of selectedCount for narrow screens; sits in the claim bar next to the Clear and New Claim buttons — keep it very short.

Shoebox.selectPrompt.source: Select receipts below to start a claim
Shoebox.selectPrompt.context: Sticky-bar label when nothing is selected yet.

Shoebox.selectPromptShort.context: Short variant of selectPrompt for narrow screens (claim bar docked at the bottom on phones) — no 'below', the receipts may sit above the bar; keep it compact, it shares a row with the New Claim button.

Shoebox.newClaim.context: THE primary call-to-action; keep the ✨. Claims.emptyBody and Shoebox.step2 quote this wording (minus ✨) — changing it re-drafts them (test-enforced).

Shoebox.clearSelection.context: Small link-style button in the claim bar that empties the current receipt selection; one short word, 'clear' as in deselect-all — not delete.

Shoebox.selectAll.context: Short link in the claim bar that selects every not-yet-claimed receipt.

Shoebox.waitingRateLimit.context: New Claim button label while the AI provider is rate-limited.

Shoebox.readingShort.context: New Claim button label while extraction runs.

Shoebox.selectHint.source: Select a receipt first — click a ✓ below
Shoebox.selectHint.context: Claim-bar guidance after clicking New Claim with nothing selected (wide screens, bar above the grid); '✓' is the literal checkmark circle on each receipt card — keep the character.

Shoebox.selectHintShort.context: Same guidance as selectHint but on phones, where the claim bar is docked at the BOTTOM of the screen so the receipts sit above it; '✓' is the literal checkmark circle on each receipt card — keep the character.

Shoebox.readingInitial.context: Live status line when extraction starts (count not yet known). Canonical for the add-receipts dialog.

Shoebox.readingCount.source: Reading {total, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}} with AI…
Shoebox.readingCount.context: Live status line once the receipt count is known (ICU). Canonical for the add-receipts dialog.

Shoebox.readProgress.context: Live progress line: {completed} of {total} receipts done. Canonical for the add-receipts dialog.

Shoebox.manualInstead.context: Escape-hatch button during a rate-limit wait: skip the AI, type rows by hand. Canonical for the add-receipts dialog.

Shoebox.manualSetup.context: Status line while the manual (no-AI) claim is being created.

Shoebox.manualStartFailed.context: Error when that manual claim can't be created.

Shoebox.generateEnded.context: Error when the progress stream ends without a result.

Shoebox.emptyTitle.context: Empty-state heading for a brand-new user (no receipts uploaded yet).

Shoebox.emptyBody.source: Upload a photo or PDF of a receipt to get started.
Shoebox.emptyBody.context: Empty-state subline inviting the first upload.

Shoebox.step1.source: <step>1. Snap.</step> Photograph receipts the moment you buy.
Shoebox.step1.context: Onboarding step 1 of 4; <step> wraps the numbered lead word — keep the 'N. Verb.' pattern.

Shoebox.step2.source: <step>2. Batch.</step> Later, when you're ready, select receipts and hit New Claim.
Shoebox.step2.context: Onboarding step 2; <step> wraps the numbered lead word. Quotes the New Claim button — its exact translation is provided as mustContain and must appear verbatim (test-enforced).

Shoebox.step3.source: <step>3. Verify.</step> Check every row against the receipt. Fix, split, or exclude items.
Shoebox.step3.context: Onboarding step 3: the human verification pass.

Shoebox.step4.source: <step>4. Print.</step> Download the filled CFCC form with receipts attached, sign, and drop it off.
Shoebox.step4.context: Onboarding step 4; 'the filled CFCC form' means the church's official reimbursement form — Chinese says 教会报销表 / 教會報銷表 rather than the English abbreviation.

Shoebox.filterAll.context: Filter chip above the receipt wall on the Receipts page; shows every receipt. {count} = how many receipts. Keep it chip-short.

Shoebox.filterUnassigned.context: Filter chip beside 'Processed receipts': receipts not yet on any generated claim (glossary term 'not on a claim'). {count} = how many.

Shoebox.filterPdf.context: Filter chip: receipts that were uploaded as PDF files. Keep 'PDF' untranslated. {count} = how many.

Shoebox.processedSummary.context: Filter chip above the receipt wall (doubles as the count of filed receipts): receipts already on a generated claim. {count} = how many.

Shoebox.processedNote.source: Already on a generated claim. You can still pick it if part of the purchase belongs on another claim.
Shoebox.processedNote.context: Fine print under that heading: processed receipts stay selectable.

Shoebox.filterGroupAria.context: aria-label of the filter-chip row on the Receipts page (screen readers only). Short verb phrase.

Shoebox.prepareTitle.context: Heading of the per-photo dialog shown before anything uploads.

Shoebox.prepareLeft.context: Suffix after that heading when more files queue: (N left). Keep the parentheses.

Shoebox.noteLabel.context: Label of the optional description input in the prepare dialog.

Shoebox.notePlaceholder.context: Example description; 'VBS' (Vacation Bible School) stays verbatim.

Shoebox.prepareHintLocal.source: Uploads when you save. You can edit the description on the card later.
Shoebox.prepareHintLocal.context: Fine print for photos: nothing uploads until Save.

Shoebox.prepareHintUploaded.source: You can edit the description on the card later.
Shoebox.prepareHintUploaded.context: Fine print for PDFs (already uploaded when picked).

Shoebox.skipAll.context: Dialog button: upload ALL queued photos without descriptions. It skips the notes, not the uploads.

Shoebox.quotaRingAria.source: Retrying in about {seconds, plural, one {# second} other {# seconds}}
Shoebox.quotaRingAria.context: aria-label of the countdown ring during a rate-limit wait (ICU seconds).

Shoebox.searchPill.context: Tappable pill at the top of the Receipts page that opens search pre-filtered to receipts. Placeholder-style, ends with ellipsis.

Shoebox.openGoneToast.source: That receipt is no longer in your Receipts.
Shoebox.openGoneToast.context: Toast when a ?open=<id> deep link targets a receipt that no longer exists.

Shoebox.step4Esign.source: <step>4. Finish.</step> Print and sign the filled CFCC form — or e-sign it right from your phone.
Shoebox.step4Esign.context: First-run guide step 4 variant shown ONLY to users inside the e-sign rollout. Outcome-neutral: both endings named, no setup CTA. Others keep Shoebox.step4.

Viewer.ariaTitle.context: aria-label of the full-screen receipt viewer dialog; {name} is the file name.

Viewer.openNewTab.context: Tooltip + aria of the ↗ icon button.

Viewer.closeTitle.context: Tooltip of ✕; 'Esc' is the keyboard key name — keep it.

Viewer.previewUnavailable.source: Preview unavailable — use ↗ to open the PDF
Viewer.previewUnavailable.context: Empty state when a PDF preview fails; ↗ refers to the open-in-new-tab icon — keep it.

Viewer.rendering.context: Spinner text while the server rasterizes a PDF. Canonical — the inline PDF preview auto-copies it.

Viewer.pageAlt.context: Alt text of each rendered PDF page; {name} file name, {page} number.

Viewer.omitted.source: +{omitted, plural, one {# more page not shown — use ↗ to open the full PDF.} other {# more pages not shown — use ↗ to open the full PDF.}}
Viewer.omitted.context: Note when a long PDF is truncated (ICU); ↗ refers to the icon — keep it.

Viewer.editTitle.context: Tooltip of that button. Keep short.

Viewer.editButton.context: Auto-copied from Review.editPhotoButton — translate that key.

Viewer.zoomOut.context: Tooltip + aria of the − zoom button.

Viewer.zoomIn.context: Tooltip + aria of the + zoom button.

Viewer.resetZoom.context: Tooltip + aria of the reset-zoom button.

Viewer.fitImage.context: Tooltip + aria of the review screen's zoom-preset button when pressing it will shrink the receipt photo so the whole image fits its frame. Short verb phrase.

Viewer.fitHeight.context: Tooltip + aria of the zoom-preset button when pressing it will scale the receipt photo so its full height fits the frame (width may overflow). Short verb phrase.

Viewer.fitWidth.context: Tooltip + aria of the zoom-preset button when pressing it will scale the receipt photo so its full width fits the frame (height may overflow). Short verb phrase.

Viewer.zoom2x.context: Tooltip + aria of the zoom-preset button when pressing it will zoom to twice the fit scale; '2×' is the multiplier — keep it.

Viewer.zoomLevel.context: Zoom badge between the buttons; {percent} is a number — keep the %.

ManualEntry.title.context: Dialog heading when the AI could not read a receipt and the user types it in.

ManualEntry.intro.source: The AI couldn't read {name}. Type in what's printed on it so you can verify the row.
ManualEntry.intro.context: Dialog intro; <strong> wraps {name}, the receipt's file name.

ManualEntry.openPdf.context: Fallback link when the browser can't embed the PDF.

ManualEntry.merchant.context: Field label: store/vendor name as printed on the receipt. One word.

ManualEntry.merchantPlaceholder.context: Example merchant; a recognizable real store name works best.

ManualEntry.purchaseDate.context: Field label for the date printed on the receipt.

ManualEntry.optional.context: Grey suffix after a field label; keep the parentheses.

ManualEntry.printedTotal.context: Field label; ($) = amount entered in dollars — keep the $.

ManualEntry.refunded.context: Field label for the refunded amount; keep the ($).

ManualEntry.amountPlaceholder.context: Numeric placeholder for money inputs; usually kept as 0.00.

ManualEntry.summary.context: Field label for the short list of items bought.

ManualEntry.summaryPlaceholder.source: e.g. Paper towels, snack variety pack, folding table

ManualEntry.rowAmount.context: Computed net line under the fields; <strong> wraps {amount}, a pre-formatted $ value.

ManualEntry.totalMinusRefunded.context: Parenthetical appended to rowAmount only when a refund exists. Keep the LEADING SPACE and the − sign.

ManualEntry.later.context: Secondary button: close the dialog and fill the row in later.

ImageEditor.title.context: Dialog heading of the rotate/crop tool.

ImageEditor.introRestoring.source: Showing the originally uploaded image. Save to keep it, or Cancel to leave the current one.
ImageEditor.introRestoring.context: Intro while previewing the original image restore.

ImageEditor.introLocal.source: Straighten the photo and drag the box to trim away the background.
ImageEditor.introLocal.context: Intro in the pre-upload flow: the edit happens on-device.

ImageEditor.introStored.source: Straighten the photo and drag the box to trim away the background. Saving replaces the stored image.
ImageEditor.introStored.context: Intro when editing an already-uploaded photo.

ImageEditor.reset.context: Button clearing the pending edit / staging the original. One word.

ImageEditor.resetTitle.source: Reset to the originally uploaded image

ImageEditor.editedAlt.context: Alt text of the photo being edited.

ImageEditor.loadFailed.source: Could not load the receipt image
ImageEditor.loadFailed.context: Error when the image can't be loaded into the editor.

ImageEditor.editFailed.context: Fallback error when saving the edit fails.

ImageEditor.saveDisabledTitle.source: Rotate or draw a crop first
ImageEditor.saveDisabledTitle.context: Tooltip on the disabled Save button before any change was made.

Review.loadFailed.context: Error when the claim fetch fails.

Review.backToClaims.context: Link inside that error banner, back to the claims list.

Review.updateFailed.context: Generic fallback when a row/claim edit fails.

Review.mergeFailed.context: Fallback when merging split rows fails.

Review.title.context: Page heading; the Draft/Generated status pill renders right after it.

Review.instruction.source: Check each amount against what you actually paid, pick a ministry, then check it off.
Review.instruction.context: One-liner under the heading describing the verification job.

Review.receiptHeader.context: Per-receipt group header; {index} is 1-based, {label} is 'Merchant — date' or a file name.

Review.headerNote.context: Suffix after the header showing the user's note; keep the '· ' separator.

Review.removeDisabledTitle.source: This is the only receipt — discard the claim instead
Review.removeDisabledTitle.context: Tooltip of the disabled Remove button when it's the claim's only receipt.

Review.removeTitle.source: Remove receipt from claim (back to the Receipts page)
Review.removeTitle.context: Tooltip of the enabled Remove button; the receipt goes back to the Receipts page.

Review.removeButton.context: Button removing a receipt from the claim; keep the ✕. Keep short.

Review.derivation.context: Banner explaining the AI's net amount; all three {args} are pre-formatted $ values. Keep the − and → symbols.

Review.editPhotoTitle.source: Rotate or crop this receipt photo
Review.editPhotoTitle.context: Tooltip of the rotate/crop button on the claim review screen.

Review.editPhotoButton.context: Rotate/crop button label; keep the ✂. Canonical — the viewer button auto-copies it.

Review.receiptNote.context: Note line above a single-receipt claim's rows; <strong> wraps {note}, the user's own text.

Review.aiFailed.source: ⚠ The AI couldn't read this receipt.
Review.aiFailed.context: Warning banner when extraction failed for this receipt; keep the ⚠.

Review.enterDetails.context: Button in that banner opening the manual-entry dialog. Keep short.

Review.subtotal.context: Label before the per-receipt subtotal amount; keep the trailing colon.

Review.claimTotal.context: Label of the claim's grand-total row.

Review.verifiedProgress.context: Progress text next to the bar; keep the '{verified} / {total}' shape.

Review.verifyAll.context: Small button in the verify-progress row that marks every remaining row verified. Keep the ✓; 'verify' = 核对 as in verifiedProgress.

Review.verifyAllConfirm.source: Mark {count, plural, one {# row} other {# rows}} as verified? Check each description, ministry, and amount against its receipt first.
Review.verifyAllConfirm.context: Body of the verify-all confirmation dialog; 'verified' = 核对 like verifiedProgress.

Review.verifyAllButton.source: Verify {count, plural, one {# row} other {# rows}}
Review.verifyAllButton.context: Confirm button of the verify-all dialog; {count} = rows about to be marked verified.

Review.profileIncomplete.source: Your name and mailing address are still missing from your profile — they are printed on the reimbursement form.
Review.profileIncomplete.context: Amber banner above the claim action bar; pairs with the 'Add name & address' link (profileIncompleteCta).

Review.profileIncompleteCta.context: Short link label in the profile banner, leads to the profile page.

Review.profileIncompleteShort.context: Tooltip on the disabled Print/E-sign buttons while the profile is missing name/address.

Review.amountRestored.source: Couldn't read that amount — restored the previous value.
Review.amountRestored.context: Inline note after an unreadable amount entry was reverted to the stored value.

Review.descriptionRestored.source: A description is required — restored the previous text.
Review.descriptionRestored.context: Inline note after an emptied row description was reverted (each row needs a description).

Review.generatedFrozen.context: Status line replacing the progress bar once the claim is generated.

Review.addReceipts.context: Action-bar button opening the add-receipts dialog; keep the full-width ＋.

Review.discard.context: Action-bar button that deletes the draft claim; keep it to one or two words.

Review.revert.context: Action-bar button on generated claims: back to editable draft. Keep the ↩.

Review.chooseMinistryFirst.context: Tooltip on the disabled verify button AND the gated PDF button.

Review.buildingPdf.context: PDF button label while busy — covers both generating a new PDF and downloading the already-signed packet, so keep it generic (not “generating”). Keep the ellipsis.

Review.printAction.context: Finish action on the review action bar: generates/downloads the packet to print & hand-sign. Sole primary when e-sign is off; secondary beside the E-sign primary when it's on. Terse, no emoji.

Review.esignAction.context: Primary finish action on the e-sign action bar: opens the electronic-signature ceremony. Terse (pairs with the Print secondary).

Review.addReceiptShort.context: Compact mobile label for the Add-receipts button in the action bar (desktop shows the fuller Review.addReceipts). Keep the leading + and singular noun.

Review.esignFinishHint.source: Finish this claim — print, or e-sign.
Review.esignFinishHint.context: One-line hint on the action bar for e-sign users on a single-receipt draft (desktop only), naming the two finish paths.

Review.revertConfirm.source: Revert this claim to draft? Only do this if you haven’t turned in the printed form yet. Rows become editable again, and the receipts leave “processed”.
Review.revertConfirm.context: In-app confirmation dialog before reverting. Quotes the 'processed' status word — its exact translation is provided as mustContain and must appear verbatim (test-enforced).

Review.revertConfirmButton.context: One-word destructive button in the revert-claim confirmation dialog, paired with Common.cancel; a verb (undo the freeze, back to draft), matching Review.revert's term.

Review.removeConfirm.source: Remove this receipt from the claim? Its rows are deleted and the receipt goes back to the Receipts page.
Review.removeConfirm.context: In-app confirmation dialog before removing a receipt from the claim; mentions the Receipts page by name — match Shoebox.title's wording.

Review.removeConfirmButton.context: One-word destructive button in the remove-receipt confirmation dialog, paired with Common.cancel; a verb, matching Review.removeButton's term.

Review.discardConfirm.source: Discard this draft claim? Receipts go back to the Receipts page.
Review.discardConfirm.context: In-app confirmation dialog before deleting the draft claim; mentions the Receipts page by name — match Shoebox.title's wording.

Review.deleteFailed.context: Error when deleting the claim fails.

Review.pdfFailed.context: Fallback error when PDF generation fails.

Review.suggestionFailed.context: Fallback error when the AI suggestion call fails.

Review.fanOutSet.source: Set {label} on {count, plural, one {# row} other {# rows}}
Review.fanOutSet.context: Undo-toast after a claim-wide ministry change; {label} is Review.quotedValue or Review.noMinistry; ICU row count.

Review.quotedValue.context: Quote wrapper for a ministry name inside fanOutSet; use the script's quote marks (“” / 「」).

Review.noMinistry.context: Slots into fanOutSet's {label} when the ministry was cleared. Lowercase fragment, not a sentence.

Review.guideSubtitle.source: Search by account number or name.

Review.guideEmpty.source: No category matches — you can still type it under “Other…”.

Review.undo.context: Button on the fan-out toast and suggestion banner. One word.

Review.dismissAria.context: aria-label of the ✕ on the toast.

Review.switchAndSplit.context: Primary button of that dialog: flip the mode, then open the split dialog.

Review.modeSwitchTitle.source: Use one ministry for the whole claim?
Review.modeSwitchTitle.context: Dialog heading when switching a multi-ministry claim to one ministry.

Review.modeSwitchBody.source: Every row will be set to {value} — the ministry most rows already use.
Review.modeSwitchBody.context: Dialog body; <strong>{value} is the adopted ministry. The ICU '=1' branch is DELIBERATELY EMPTY — the clause about N different ministries only appears when more than one is picked.

Review.modeSwitchBodyNoMinistry.source: Rows keep no ministry until you pick one at the top.
Review.modeSwitchBodyNoMinistry.context: Variant of that body when no row has a ministry yet.

Review.modeSwitchUnverify.source: {count, plural, one {# verified row} other {# verified rows}} will need re-verifying.
Review.modeSwitchUnverify.context: Amber warning inside that dialog: verified rows lose their check (ICU).

Review.panelTitleSingle.context: Heading of the ministry panel in single-ministry mode.

Review.panelTitle.context: Heading of the same panel in multi mode.

Review.modeOne.context: Left segment of the mode toggle; keep short. Review.multiHint quotes this wording (test-enforced).

Review.modeMultiple.context: Right segment of the mode toggle. One word if possible.

Review.descPlaceholder.source: What’s this claim for? e.g. “snacks for the youth retreat”
Review.descPlaceholder.context: Placeholder of the claim-description input; keep the quoted example phrasing.

Review.suggestTitle.source: Let the AI suggest a ministry & event from your description — you still apply it
Review.suggestTitle.context: Tooltip of the Suggest button: the AI proposes, the human applies.

Review.thinking.context: Suggest button label while the AI call runs. Keep the ellipsis.

Review.suggest.context: The AI-suggestion button; keep the ✨. Keep short.

Review.suggestionApplied.context: Suggestion-banner prefix after applying; <strong>{value} is the ministry — event.

Review.suggestionSuggested.context: Suggestion-banner prefix before applying; <strong>{value} as above.

Review.applyAllRows.context: Banner button on multi-receipt claims: mirror the suggestion onto every row.

Review.apply.context: Same button on single-receipt claims. One word.

Review.dismiss.context: Banner link that discards the suggestion. One word.

Review.noConfidentMatch.source: No confident match — pick a ministry below.
Review.noConfidentMatch.context: Banner when the AI declines to pick from the chart of accounts.

Review.pickMinistry.context: Empty first <option> of every ministry select; keep the '— … —' dash style.

Review.otherOption.context: Last <option>, opening a free-text ministry input; keep the ellipsis.

Review.claimMinistryAria.context: aria-label of the CLAIM-level ministry select. MUST stay distinct from Review.ministryAria (row-level) — tests and screen readers rely on it.

Review.customClaimMinistryAria.context: aria-label of the claim-level free-text ministry input; keep distinct from Review.customMinistryAria.

Review.eventPlaceholder.context: Placeholder of the event inputs; keep the (optional) suffix. An event example: a retreat, VBS.

Review.claimEventAria.context: aria-label of the claim-level event input; keep distinct from Review.eventAria.

Review.appliedEveryRow.source: Applied to every row — you still confirm each amount below.
Review.appliedEveryRow.context: Fine print under the claim-level controls: values mirror to rows, amounts still need per-row confirmation.

Review.aiOptional.context: Tiny hint beside a form field meaning it's optional. One word, lowercase.

Review.aiSub.source: Describe the claim in a sentence — I’ll pick the budget category & event.

Review.send.context: Button that sends the typed message to the AI assistant. One word, imperative verb.

Review.candidatesLead.source: A few of these could fit — which budget line?

Review.somethingElse.context: Last option in a picker: the user will type their own answer instead. Trailing ellipsis.

Review.somethingElsePlaceholder.source: Add a detail — e.g. “it was VBS, not a retreat”

Review.pickManuallyEscape.source: None of these — pick a category below

Review.multiHint.source: Each row picks its own ministry below. Switch to “One ministry” to set them all at once.
Review.multiHint.context: Fine print in multi mode. Quotes the One-ministry toggle — its exact translation is provided as mustContain and must appear verbatim (test-enforced).

Review.rowDescPlaceholder.context: Placeholder of each row's description textarea.

Review.rowDescAria.context: aria-label of that textarea. One word.

Review.badgeTitle.source: Ministry applies claim-wide — set it at the top
Review.badgeTitle.context: Tooltip on a row's ministry badge in single mode: change it at the top.

Review.ministrySetAbove.context: Badge text when the row has no ministry yet; ↑ points at the panel above — keep it.

Review.ministryAria.context: aria-label of the ROW-level ministry select. MUST stay distinct from Review.claimMinistryAria. One word.

Review.customMinistryAria.context: aria-label of the row-level free-text ministry input.

Review.eventAria.context: aria-label of the row-level event input. One word.

Review.amountAria.context: aria-label of the row's dollar-amount input; a literal $ sits before the box. One word.

Review.refundBadge.context: Tiny red badge on negative rows. English is uppercase for loudness; keep it very short.

Review.splitRowTitle.context: Tooltip of the Split button: divide one receipt between ministries.

Review.splitButton.context: Row action; keep the ⑂. Glossary: 拆分. Keep short.

Review.mergeBlockedTitle.source: Restore the excluded row before merging
Review.mergeBlockedTitle.context: Tooltip of Merge when the row above is excluded.

Review.mergeTitle.source: Merge back into the row above (undo split)
Review.mergeTitle.context: Tooltip of Merge: fold this split half back into the row above.

Review.mergeButton.context: Row action; keep the ⤴. Keep short.

Review.restoreTitle.context: Tooltip of the exclude button when the row is already excluded.

Review.excludeTitle.context: Tooltip of the exclude button: drop a personal / non-reimbursable item.

Review.restoreButton.context: Row action un-excluding an item; keep the ↩.

Review.excludeButton.context: Row action excluding an item; keep the 🗑.

Review.coachHint.source: One receipt can cover more than one ministry — or include personal items. Use Split on a row to send part of it elsewhere, or leave it off the claim.

Review.nudgeVerify.context: Pulsing hint pointing (→) at the confirm button when it's ready to click.

Review.nudgeSetAbove.source: Set the ministry at the top, then verify →
Review.nudgeSetAbove.context: Same hint on single-ministry claims when no ministry is set yet; keep the →.

Review.nudgePick.context: Same hint on multi-ministry claims; keep the →.

Review.verifyTitleVerified.context: Tooltip of the confirm button once verified (clicking undoes).

Review.verifyTitle.source: Click to certify that description, ministry, and amount are correct.
Review.verifyTitle.context: Tooltip of the confirm button: this click is the human attestation.

Review.looksCorrect.context: THE verification button on every row — 'I checked this against the receipt'. Keep short; a ✓ checkbox renders before it.

Review.splitAmountQuestion.source: How much of the {amount} is for something else?

Review.splitForPersonalSub.source: Not reimbursable — kept off the claim.

Review.splitModeSwitchNote.source: One ministry is set for the whole claim — assigning a different one here switches it to multiple ministries.

Review.splitFailed.context: Fallback error when the split fails.

Review.splitConfirm.context: Primary button of the split dialog. One word; pairs with Review.splitButton.

Review.underSignatureFrozen.source: Under signature — packet and rows are frozen.
Review.underSignatureFrozen.context: Note replacing the row toolbar once a claim is submitted for e-signature: the PDF packet and rows can no longer change.

Review.downloadSigned.context: Download button once a claim is under signature — downloads the archived signed packet instead of regenerating. Keep the ⬇.

Review.revertConfirmSigned.source: Revert this claim to draft? Every signature collected so far becomes void, and approval starts over.
Review.revertConfirmSigned.context: in-app confirmation dialog before reverting a claim that has e-signatures: warns that collected signatures become void and approval restarts.

Review.retiredOption.context: Select option label for a row's stored category whose catalog entry was archived; {value} = the composed code+name. 'retired' = archived, not deleted.

Review.esignSetupHint.source: Finish this claim — print it, or set up signing.
Review.esignSetupHint.context: Desktop gutter hint on the claim action bar for users who haven't set up e-sign; names the button verbatim (“set up signing”).

Review.esignSetupAction.context: Secondary action-bar button where E-sign would be, for users who haven't set up. Opens an explanation callout — never the ceremony. Same-value group with Identity.enrollButton.

Review.esignWaitingAction.context: Action-bar button label while the user's enrollment awaits in-person confirmation. Same wording as the Identity.chipPending status chip (same-value group).

Review.esignWaitingAria.source: E-signing: waiting for in-person confirmation — details
Review.esignWaitingAria.context: Screen-reader label for the waiting-state button (the visible label alone reads as a status; this adds the action).

Review.esignSetupCalloutTitle.source: E-sign this claim? One-time setup first
Review.esignSetupCalloutTitle.context: Title of the callout the demoted e-sign button opens (user hasn't started setup).

Review.esignSetupCalloutNull.source: You'll sign in with Google again, set up in about two minutes, and then two members (or one approver) confirm it's really you in person — usually at the next service.
Review.esignSetupCalloutNull.context: Callout body (not-started state). Same two-part expectation as Home.esignNudgeNull.

Review.esignSetupCalloutPendingTitle.context: Callout title while enrollment awaits the in-person confirmation.

Review.esignSetupCalloutPending.source: Show your QR code to two members (or one approver) at church — after that, claims like this can be e-signed.

Review.esignSetupCalloutRevokedTitle.source: Electronic signing is turned off for you
Review.esignSetupCalloutRevokedTitle.context: Callout title when the user's signing identity was revoked. Neutral, no pitch.

Review.esignSetupCalloutRevoked.source: Your signing identity was turned off. See your profile for details.
Review.esignSetupCalloutRevoked.context: Callout body for the revoked state — the profile card owns the full story.

Review.esignSetupCalloutPaper.source: You can still hand this claim in on paper — download the PDF now and print it at church or wherever's easy.
Review.esignSetupCalloutPaper.context: Paper-reassurance line in the callout. Outcome-worded on purpose (download now, print wherever) — many members have no printer at home.

Review.esignSetupCalloutCta.context: Callout CTA → profile signing card. Same-value group with Identity.enrollButton.

Review.esignSetupCalloutQrCta.context: Callout CTA in the pending state → the QR on the profile. Same-value group with NavBar.showYourCode.

Esign.submitForApproval.context: Primary button opening the submit-for-approval signing dialog. Keep the ✍️ prefix.

Esign.panelSubmitted.context: Panel heading while the claim waits for the approver.

Esign.panelRejected.context: Panel heading after the approver rejected the claim.

Esign.panelApproved.context: Panel heading after approval, before the treasurer pays.

Esign.panelPaid.context: Panel heading once paid (no check number recorded).

Esign.panelPaidWithCheck.context: Panel heading once paid; {checkNumber} is the check number the treasurer typed.

Esign.approverComment.context: Shows the approver's rejection comment; {comment} is their text. Keep the quotation marks around it.

Esign.certificateLink.context: Link to download the PDF approval certificate. Keep the 📜.

Esign.withdrawConfirm.source: Withdraw this submission so you can pick a different approver? You can submit again right after from the claim’s E-sign button.
Esign.withdrawConfirm.context: in-app confirmation dialog before withdrawing a submission to pick a different approver.

Esign.withdrawConfirmButton.context: One-word destructive button in the withdraw-submission confirmation dialog, paired with Common.cancel; a verb, matching Esign.withdrawButton's term.

Esign.withdrawButton.context: Button to withdraw the pending submission / change approver. No icon — the old ↪ was mistaken for a refresh arrow.

Esign.orphanNote.source: This claim still has an open approval request from an earlier submission. Withdraw it before you can submit this claim for approval again.
Esign.orphanNote.context: Recovery notice on a generated/draft claim that was submitted for approval, then reverted without withdrawing — the earlier approval request is still open on the signature ledger and blocks resubmission until withdrawn. 'claim' = a reimbursement claim (报销单).

Esign.orphanWithdrawConfirm.source: Withdraw the open approval request? This clears the earlier submission so you can send this claim for approval again.
Esign.orphanWithdrawConfirm.context: Confirm-dialog body for the orphan-recovery withdraw. Confirming appends a signed WITHDRAW that closes the still-open approval request so the claim can be submitted for approval again. Pairs with the button Esign.withdrawConfirmButton ('Withdraw').

Esign.resubmit.context: Button to reopen the signing dialog after a rejection. Keep the ✍️.

Esign.checkStatus.context: Button on a submitted/approved claim: asks the server whether the approver (or payment) has happened yet. NOT the document-integrity re-verification and NOT a software-update check — use progress/inquiry vocabulary (查詢/查看進度), never 驗證/核對/更新.

Esign.checkStatusBusy.context: In-flight line while the Check status request runs. Same progress-inquiry vocabulary as the button; keep the ellipsis.

Esign.checkStatusNoChange.source: No changes yet — still waiting for {name}.
Esign.checkStatusNoChange.context: Reassuring inline result (not an error) when Check status finds nothing new on a submitted claim; {name} is the approver's display name. Echo the tone of Esign.waitingFor.

Esign.checkStatusNoChangePayment.source: No changes yet — still waiting for payment.
Esign.checkStatusNoChangePayment.context: Same as checkStatusNoChange but for an approved claim waiting on the treasurer's payment.

Esign.checkStatusNoChangeGeneric.context: Fallback no-change result when the approver's name is unavailable. Short.

Esign.checkStatusCheckedAt.context: Muted suffix after the no-change result; {time} is a localized clock time. Restates on every press so repeat checks visibly change. Keep the parentheses.

Esign.checkStatusFailed.source: Couldn't check status — please try again.
Esign.checkStatusFailed.context: Inline error when the Check status request fails (network). Must not sound like a permissions problem (not 无法查看这张报销单); invite a retry.

Esign.verifyAgain.context: Button inside the Audit details disclosure that re-runs cryptographic integrity verification of the packet. Use verification vocabulary (驗證) matching bannerOk/bannerBad — never approval (核准/批准) or line-item checking (核對) vocabulary.

Esign.verifiedAtTime.context: Green confirmation after a manual Verify again pass; {time} is a localized clock time. Integrity result only — must not read as claim approval (驗證無誤-style, never 通過/批准). Keep the ✓.

Esign.verifyUnavailable.source: Couldn't check right now — connection problem. Please try again later.
Esign.verifyUnavailable.context: Neutral (amber) note when chain verification failed for transport reasons — offline, ledger unreachable. Deliberately NOT the red tamper alert: must read as a connectivity hiccup, not a document problem.

Esign.withdrawRaceApproved.source: {name} already approved this claim, so it can no longer be withdrawn.
Esign.withdrawRaceApproved.context: Shown when a withdraw attempt lost the race with the approver approving; {name} is the approver. Explains why withdrawal is refused. Approval verb differs by script: 核准 (Hant) / 批准 (Hans).

Esign.withdrawRaceRejected.source: {name} already returned this claim, so there is nothing to withdraw.
Esign.withdrawRaceRejected.context: Shown when a withdraw attempt lost the race with the approver rejecting; {name} is the approver. 'Returned' = sent back for fixes (退回), matching the rejection flow's wording.

Esign.verifyingChain.context: Progress line while the browser re-checks all signatures. Plain wording (not "verifying the signature chain") keeps verifiability low-key. Keep the ellipsis.

Esign.failClosed.source: Signing is disabled until everything checks out.
Esign.failClosed.context: Amber note under the banner when verification hasn't fully passed: the Approve/Reject buttons stay disabled.

Esign.openPacketButton.context: Button opening the full reimbursement PDF (form pages + appended receipts) the approver/treasurer reviews before signing. "reimbursement" not "verified" — the packet's the point, not its verifiability. Keep the 📄.

Esign.openApprovedPacketButton.source: 🖋️ Open approved packet — with approver's signature

Esign.openOriginalPacketLink.context: Link that opens the originally-submitted (un-stamped) PDF version. '(as submitted)' says which version.

Esign.submitDialogTitle.context: Title of the signing dialog. Must read the same as the ✍️ Submit for approval button (without the emoji).

Esign.submitIntro.source: You're asking for approval of this claim ({amount}). Place your signature on the form, pick who should approve it, and sign. If the claim changes later, your signature stops counting and approval starts over.
Esign.submitIntro.context: Explains the submit ceremony. {amount} is the claim total. Plain, non-technical wording.

Esign.openingForm.context: Placeholder while the PDF loads into the signing view. Keep the ellipsis.

Esign.prepFailed.source: Couldn’t load the form — check your connection and try again.
Esign.prepFailed.context: Error box in a signing ceremony when loading the PDF/signature anchor failed; paired with the Retry link.

Esign.retryButton.context: One-word retry link inside the ceremony load-failure box.

Esign.noEligibleApprovers.source: No approver is available to pick right now. Check with your treasurer — and you can still print this claim and hand it in on paper today.
Esign.noEligibleApprovers.context: Submit-dialog notice when the approver list is empty. Plain register (no roster jargon) and names the recovery: paper still works today.

Esign.placeSignatureHint.source: Press and hold on the form above to place your signature first.
Esign.placeSignatureHint.context: Hint above the disabled Sign/Approve button until the user press-holds the form preview to stamp their signature.

Esign.approverLabel.context: Label of the approver dropdown in the signing dialog.

Esign.approverPlaceholder.context: Empty first option of that dropdown. Keep the ellipsis.

Esign.typedNameLabel.source: Type your full name to sign
Esign.typedNameLabel.context: Label of the type-your-full-name field — typing the name is part of the legal signature.

Esign.consentSummary.context: Collapsed <details> summary hiding the legal consent text.

Esign.consentEnglishNote.source: The legally binding consent text is the English version below.
Esign.consentEnglishNote.context: Small note above the consent document explaining that the binding text is the English original (it is hash-bound and cannot be translated).

Esign.intentAffirmation.source: I intend this to be my signature.
Esign.intentAffirmation.context: Checkbox label the signer must tick — a legal affirmation of intent to sign. Keep it a serious, first-person statement.

Esign.signAndSubmit.context: Confirm button of the submit ceremony (signs and sends to the approver).

Esign.signing.context: Busy label while a signature is being created. Keep the ellipsis.

Esign.submissionFailed.context: Fallback error if the submit ceremony fails without a specific message.

Esign.ceremonyFailed.context: Fallback error if an approve/reject/paid ceremony fails without a specific message.

Esign.loadFailed.context: Fallback error when a list (approvals, finance, vouch) fails to load.

Esign.connectBody.source: Sign in with your church Google account to sign securely. You only need to do this once on this device.

Esign.connectButton.context: Button to connect this device's signing session (unlock the signing key for a ceremony). Imperative.

Esign.connecting.context: Transient status shown while the signing session connects. Trailing ellipsis.

Esign.connectBlocked.source: Your browser blocked the sign-in window. Tap “Connect signing” again to allow it.

Esign.itemsCount.context: Fallback list-row description when a claim has no description: its line-item count.

Esign.metaSubmitted.context: Dates line under a claim row in the approver inbox and finance queue. {date} is a formatted date or the word Today/Yesterday. Reads e.g. “Submitted Jul 3”.

Esign.metaApproved.context: Dates line under a claim row; pairs with metaSubmitted. {date} is a date or Today/Yesterday. “Approved” = the approval was recorded.

Esign.metaRejected.context: Dates line under a decided claim row. {date} is a date or Today/Yesterday. “Rejected” = the approval was declined.

Esign.metaPaid.context: Dates line under a paid claim row in the finance queue. {date} is a date or Today/Yesterday. “Paid” = payment recorded, not a fee.

Esign.bannerOk.source: ✓ Everything checks out — this is the genuine paperwork, unchanged.
Esign.bannerOk.context: Reassurance line at the top of the Audit details disclosure (not shown on the main path — a clean chain stays quiet). Written for non-technical members. Keep the leading ✓.

Esign.bannerBad.source: ✗ Something doesn't check out. Don't sign — ask the person who sent this (details below for whoever helps you).
Esign.bannerBad.context: Red alert — the ONLY chain banner shown on the main path, and only when a check fails: tells the member not to sign and to ask for help. Keep the leading ✗.

Esign.auditDetails.context: Collapsed <details> summary hiding cryptographic detail (fingerprints, hashes). Used on several e-sign screens.

Esign.pillRootPinned.context: Green pill inside audit details: the church root key matched the pinned value. {by} says what pinned it (config/device).

Esign.pillRootFailed.context: Red pill: the root key did NOT match. Keep it alarming (capitals in English).

Esign.pillChain.context: Pill label for the signature-chain check.

Esign.pillPacket.context: Pill label for the packet-bytes hash check.

Esign.anomaliesHeader.source: {count, plural, one {# invalid event on the ledger (kept visible, never hidden):} other {# invalid events on the ledger (kept visible, never hidden):}}
Esign.anomaliesHeader.context: Audit list header when invalid ledger events exist. {count} plural. 'Kept visible' = the app never hides tampering.

Esign.independentCheck.source: Independent check: download the certificate and run scripts/verify-bundle.mjs with the church's published root fingerprint.
Esign.independentCheck.context: Audit note telling a technical auditor how to verify offline. Keep the script name inside <code> untranslated.

Esign.signerFingerprints.context: Audit list header for each signer's key fingerprint.

Esign.unknownMember.context: Placeholder when a signer's key is not on the roster (lowercase, inline).

Esign.blockSubmitted.context: Signature-block title: the requester's submit signature. {seq} is the submission attempt number.

Esign.blockApproved.context: Signature-block title when the decision was approval.

Esign.blockRejected.context: Signature-block title when the decision was rejection.

Esign.blockPaid.context: Signature-block title for the treasurer's payment signature.

Esign.checkNumber.context: Line under the payment signature; {number} is the check number.

Esign.unknownSigner.context: Shown instead of a name when the signer isn't identifiable.

Esign.tapToSign.context: Pulsing yellow tab ON the document's signature line — pressing and HOLDING it stamps the signature (the legally required act). Keep the ✍️ and keep it short.

Esign.holdingToSign.context: Label shown on the sign tab while the signer is mid-hold and a progress ring is filling. Keep it short.

Esign.signedDragHint.source: Signed. Drag your signature if you want it somewhere else.
Esign.signedDragHint.context: Hint after the signature is stamped: it can be dragged to reposition.

Esign.holdTabHint.source: Press and hold the yellow tab to sign on the line.
Esign.holdTabHint.context: Hint before stamping: tells the signer to press and hold the yellow tab.

Esign.dragFineTune.context: Small hint below the document while the stamp is placed.

Esign.removeSignature.context: Small link that removes the stamped signature (returns to the hold-to-sign state).

Esign.appearsAfterTap.source: Your signature appears after you hold the yellow tab.
Esign.appearsAfterTap.context: Small reassurance below the document before the signer has held the tab.

Esign.zoomIn.context: aria-label/tooltip for the zoom-in (+) button on the document preview.

Esign.zoomOut.context: aria-label/tooltip for the zoom-out (−) button on the document preview.

Esign.zoomReset.context: aria-label/tooltip for the button (shows the current zoom %) that resets the document preview to 100%.

Esign.couldNotOpenDoc.source: Couldn't open the document to sign. {message}
Esign.couldNotOpenDoc.context: Error box when the PDF can't be rendered; {message} is the technical reason (may stay English).

Esign.openingDoc.context: Placeholder while the PDF renders. Keep the ellipsis.

Esign.docAlt.context: alt text of the rendered document image.

Esign.signatureAlt.context: alt text of the member's hand-drawn signature image.

Esign.signAbove.source: Sign above with your finger or mouse
Esign.signAbove.context: Caption under the empty drawing canvas (draw with finger or mouse).

Esign.clearPad.context: Link that wipes the drawing canvas.

Esign.waitingFor.source: Waiting for {name} to review this claim.
Esign.waitingFor.context: Line on the claim owner's panel while status is “awaiting approval”; {name} is the chosen approver's display name.

Esign.approverPausedNote.source: {name} isn't taking new approval requests right now. They can still decide this claim, or you can withdraw it and pick a different approver.
Esign.approverPausedNote.context: Amber notice on the owner's claim panel when the assigned approver has switched off NEW approval requests; they can still decide this already-assigned claim. Pairs with the withdraw/change-approver button below it.

Esign.approverIneligibleNote.source: {name} can no longer approve claims. Withdraw this submission and pick a different approver.
Esign.approverIneligibleNote.context: Amber notice on the owner's claim panel when the assigned approver lost the approver role (or signing key) and can no longer approve at all — the owner must withdraw and reassign.

Esign.approverPrefilledFrom.source: Pre-filled from the {position} default. Change it below if needed.

Esign.signedButNotSynced.source: Your signature was recorded. The page couldn’t refresh the claim’s status — it will catch up on its own. Do not sign again.
Esign.signedButNotSynced.context: Shown when a ceremony's signature IS committed to the ledger but the status display failed to update; warns against signing again.

Esign.hintPickApprover.context: Inline hint under a disabled sign button: an approver must be selected first.

Esign.hintTypeName.context: Inline hint under a disabled sign button: the typed-name field is empty.

Esign.hintAffirm.context: Inline hint under a disabled sign button: the intent-affirmation checkbox is unticked.

Esign.consentGloss.source: In short: you agree that signing in this app counts as your real, legally binding signature — the same as ink on paper. (This is a summary; the binding text is the English document below.)
Esign.consentGloss.context: Plain-language SUMMARY of the legally binding English consent text, shown above it; must say it is only a summary.

Esign.withdrawFailed.source: Could not withdraw the submission — nothing changed. Try again.
Esign.withdrawFailed.context: Error in the withdraw-submission dialog when nothing was withdrawn.

Esign.makeChanges.context: Primary button on a rejected claim: reopen it for editing (revert to draft). Keep the ✏️.

Esign.reviseConfirm.source: Reopen this claim for editing? Every signature collected so far becomes void, and approval starts over when you resubmit.
Esign.reviseConfirm.context: Confirmation body before reopening a rejected claim for editing; signatures become void.

Esign.reviseConfirmButton.context: Confirm button of the reopen-for-editing dialog. Short.

Esign.reviseFailed.context: Error when the reopen-for-editing (revert) call fails.

Esign.waitingDays.source: · waiting {days, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}
Esign.waitingDays.context: Aging cue appended to a queue row's date line, amber; leading dot separates it from the dates.

Esign.alreadyDecidedNote.source: This claim has already been decided — the list will update in a moment.
Esign.alreadyDecidedNote.context: Amber note in a ceremony when the claim was decided elsewhere while it was open.

Esign.assignedElsewhereNote.source: This claim is assigned to a different approver.
Esign.assignedElsewhereNote.context: Amber note in the approver ceremony when the open claim is assigned to a different approver.

Esign.undoStroke.context: Small link under the signature pad removing only the last drawn stroke (vs Clear which wipes all). Short.

Esign.stalledNote.source: Waiting for a signature for {days, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}. Notifications are often missed — phones get muted, batteries die. You can withdraw and pick a different approver at any time.
Esign.stalledNote.context: Amber note on a claim waiting >7 days for a signature. MUST stay de-personalized and charitable (phones mute, batteries die) — never imply the approver is ignoring the owner. ICU plural on {days}.

Esign.notAttestedNull.source: To e-sign, first set up signing on your <link>profile</link> — then two members confirm it's really you in person.
Esign.notAttestedNull.context: Submit-dialog notice when the user never started setup. Plain register: no roster/vouch jargon; the in-person step is stated up front.

Esign.notAttestedPending.source: Almost there — show your QR code (it's on your <link>profile</link>) to two members or one approver at church. After that you can sign.
Esign.notAttestedPending.context: Submit-dialog notice while enrollment awaits the vouch — must NOT tell them to enroll again.

Esign.notAttestedRevoked.source: Your signing identity was turned off. See your <link>profile</link> for details.
Esign.notAttestedRevoked.context: Submit-dialog notice for a revoked identity. Neutral, profile-owned story.

Approvals.title.context: Page title of the approver's inbox; also the nav link.

Approvals.subtitle.source: Open a claim to check it and sign.
Approvals.subtitle.context: One-line instruction under the inbox title.

Approvals.empty.source: Nothing is waiting for your signature. Owners can withdraw or reassign a request at any time — this is routine.
Approvals.empty.context: Empty state of the approvals inbox. CAUSE-NEUTRAL AND NORMALIZING on purpose: with push, a withdrawn request leaves a stale notification, so this copy must read as routine workflow, never 'you were passed over'. Do not imply someone else handled it (approvals are assigned to exactly one person).

Approvals.decided.context: Section header (uppercase style) listing already-decided claims.

Approvals.commentLabel.context: Label of the comment field; a comment is mandatory only for rejection.

Approvals.rejectNeedsComment.source: Add a comment explaining the rejection
Approvals.rejectNeedsComment.context: Tooltip on the disabled Reject button until a comment is typed.

Approvals.approveAndSign.context: Approve button — approving IS signing. Keep the ✓.

Approvals.pausedNotice.source: You've turned off new approval requests. These claims were assigned to you before that — please decide them, or their owners can reassign them.
Approvals.pausedNotice.context: Banner atop the approver's inbox when they have paused new requests but still have earlier-assigned claims waiting.

Approvals.roleLostNotice.source: Your approver role has been removed. You can decline these claims so their owners can reassign them, but you can't approve them.
Approvals.roleLostNotice.context: Banner atop the approver's inbox when their approver role was revoked: rejecting (declining) is still allowed, approving is not. Also the tooltip on the disabled approve button.

Approvals.rejectConfirmTitle.context: Title of the confirmation dialog shown when an approver taps Reject. Ends with a question mark.

Approvals.rejectConfirmBody.source: Rejecting is final — you won't be able to undo it or approve this claim later. {name} will have to revise and resubmit the claim for a new approval.
Approvals.rejectConfirmBody.context: Body of the reject-confirmation dialog, warning the approver that rejection cannot be undone or re-approved by them. {name} is the claim owner's name; only the owner can revise and resubmit.

Approvals.rejectConfirmAction.context: Confirm button inside the reject-confirmation dialog — the committing, irreversible action. Keep the ✗.

Approvals.openGone.source: That claim isn’t in this list anymore — the owner may have withdrawn or reassigned it, which is routine.
Approvals.openGone.context: Shown when a deep-linked claim is no longer in the approvals list. Cause-neutral like Approvals.empty: withdrawal/reassignment is routine; never imply blame or that another approver took it.

Approvals.emptyNotSetUp.source: Claims can't be sent to you yet — <link>set up signing</link> so members can pick you as their approver.
Approvals.emptyNotSetUp.context: Approvals inbox empty state for a role-holder who never enrolled (rare transitional state). The one surface where the deficit sentence is allowed — it's their own duty page.

Approvals.emptyPendingVouch.source: Setup done — one in-person confirmation left. <link>Show your QR code</link> at church; after that members can send you claims.
Approvals.emptyPendingVouch.context: Approvals inbox empty state for a role-holder mid-re-enrollment (pending).

Approvals.emptyRevoked.source: Your signing identity was turned off. See your <link>profile</link> for details.
Approvals.emptyRevoked.context: Approvals inbox empty state for a revoked identity. Neutral.

Finance.title.context: Page title of the treasurer's queue; also the nav link.

Finance.subtitle.context: One-line description under the finance title.

Finance.empty.source: Nothing to pay right now — an approved claim may already have been handled by someone else.
Finance.empty.context: Empty state of the treasurer payment queue. Here 'already handled by someone else' IS accurate (any treasurer may pay) — that phrasing reassures after a stale notification tap.

Finance.viewCertificate.context: Button on each paid-claim row; opens that claim's approval certificate PDF in a new tab. One word, verb ("view/open"), not "a view".

Finance.checkNumberLabel.context: Label of the optional check-number field in the mark-paid ceremony.

Finance.signAndMarkPaid.context: Confirm button of the payment ceremony. Keep the 💸.

Finance.selectPacket.context: Accessible label for the checkbox that selects a paid claim’s packet for batch printing. {name} is the claimant.

Finance.selectedCount.source: {count, plural, one {# packet selected} other {# packets selected}}
Finance.selectedCount.context: Count in the floating batch-print toolbar (finance queue) showing how many paid packets the treasurer has selected.

Finance.includeReceipts.context: Toggle in the batch-print toolbar: append each packet’s receipt pages to the printout. Checkbox label.

Finance.includeCertificate.context: Toggle in the batch-print toolbar: prepend the signature certificate cover to each packet. Checkbox label.

Finance.clearSelection.context: Button in the batch-print toolbar that deselects all packets. One word.

Finance.printSelected.context: Primary button on the floating batch-print toolbar (finance queue), next to "Clear" and the "# packets selected" count — prints ONLY the checked packets, never the whole paid list. "单据包" deliberately matches the toolbar’s packet noun (Finance.selectedCount).

Finance.printing.context: Busy label on the Print selected button while the combined PDF is being built.

Finance.printFailed.source: Couldn't build the packets. Please try again.
Finance.printFailed.context: Error shown in the batch-print toolbar when building the combined PDF failed.

Finance.openGone.source: That claim isn’t in this list anymore — it may already have been paid.
Finance.openGone.context: Toast when a deep-linked claim is no longer in the finance queue.

Finance.noCheckNumberNote.source: No check number — that’s fine for non-check payments; add one if a check was written.
Finance.noCheckNumberNote.context: Small note under the empty check-number field in the mark-paid ceremony: blank is a valid choice.
