Finance.selectPaidToday.context: Text link beside the "Paid" section header on the finance page; tapping checks the rows of claims whose PAID date (not submitted/approved) is today, adding them to the selection. {count} = how many such claims exist; only rendered when count ≥ 1. "今天" must match Common.date.today, which the rows’ "Paid Today" meta line uses.

Identity.title.context: Heading of the profile-page card for electronic signing.

Identity.chipReady.context: Status chip: fully vouched, can sign.

Identity.chipPending.context: Profile signing-card status chip while enrollment awaits the in-person confirmation. Candidate-facing register: “confirmation”, never the guarantor word (see GLOSSARY vouch split). Canonical for its same-value group.

Identity.chipRevoked.context: Status chip: the signing key was revoked.

Identity.chipNone.context: Status chip: never set up signing.

Identity.loadFailed.context: Error when the signing status can't be loaded.

Identity.switchFailed.context: Error when flipping the master switch fails.

Identity.switchOn.context: Bold line of the admin's master-switch row when e-signing is on. ON is deliberately loud.

Identity.switchOff.context: Same row when off. OFF is deliberately loud.

Identity.switchOnBody.source: Members can enroll, submit, approve, and pay electronically.
Identity.switchOnBody.context: Small print under the ON state: what members can now do.

Identity.switchOffBody.source: Off for everyone (the default). Existing signed records stay verifiable.
Identity.switchOffBody.context: Small print under the OFF state: off is the default; old signed records stay verifiable.

Identity.bootstrapIntro.source: You're the person who sets up electronic signing for this church. It starts switched off, so nothing changes for members until you turn it on.
Identity.bootstrapIntro.context: Shown only to the designated first admin before setup. <b> wraps 'off'. Explains setup creates the registry with them as anchor.

Identity.bootstrapButton.context: Button that performs the one-time setup.

Identity.bootstrapBusy.context: Busy label during setup. Keep the ellipsis.

Identity.notSetUp.source: Electronic signing isn't set up for this church yet — ask your administrator.
Identity.notSetUp.context: Shown to members while the church hasn't set up e-signing.

Identity.enrollIntro.source: Sign and submit claims from your phone — no printing. You'll agree to sign electronically, draw your signature, and then two members (or one approver) confirm it's really you in person — one quick scan.
Identity.enrollIntro.context: Intro paragraph on the profile signing card before enrollment — the landing every nudge points at, so it repeats the nudges' vocabulary: members “sign and submit” (never “approve”), and the “(or one approver)” quorum option is stated.

Identity.checkingDevice.context: Placeholder while the app checks whether this browser holds the member's keys. Keep the ellipsis.

Identity.yourSignature.context: Small label above the saved hand-drawn signature image.

Identity.redraw.context: Small link to redraw the saved signature.

Identity.addSignature.context: Button when no drawn signature exists yet. Keep the ✍️.

Identity.pendingVouch.source: One last step: show this to two members (or one approver) in person. They open their Vouch tab, scan it, and confirm it's really you.
Identity.pendingVouch.context: Amber instructions above the QR while awaiting vouches. <b> wraps 'in person' and the Vouch nav-tab name (must match NavBar.vouch verbatim). Two members or one approver must scan.

Identity.roleLabel.context: Audit-details line label; the member's church role follows it.

Identity.yourFingerprint.context: Audit-details line label; the member's key fingerprint follows.

Identity.fullFingerprint.context: Parenthetical giving the untruncated fingerprint; {fp} is 64 hex chars.

Identity.rootFingerprint.context: Audit-details line label for the church's root key fingerprint.

Identity.compareNote.context: Dash-note after the root fingerprint: compare it against the value the church published.

Identity.openLink.context: Compact label on the candidate's vouching QR — a link to the /vouch URL for a voucher whose phone camera opens it directly. Replaces the previously displayed full URL.

Identity.manualFallbackTitle.context: Summary of a disclosure under the candidate's vouching QR, revealing the key fingerprint to read out when the camera can't scan.

Identity.manualFallbackBody.source: Have your voucher pick you from the list on their Vouch screen and type this key fingerprint by hand:
Identity.manualFallbackBody.context: Body of the camera-less fallback: the voucher picks the candidate from the pending list and types the full key fingerprint (never the short spoken code). Mirrors Vouch.scanIntro.

Identity.copyCode.context: Button that copies the key fingerprint to the clipboard.

Identity.copiedCode.context: Transient confirmation shown on the copy button after the fingerprint is copied.

Identity.qrAria.context: aria-label of the vouching QR code image.

Identity.consentTitle.source: Sign electronically instead of on paper
Identity.consentTitle.context: Title of the consent step of the enrollment wizard.

Identity.consentIntro.source: Please read this once — it says your electronic signature counts like an ink one, and that you can always go back to paper.
Identity.consentIntro.context: Friendly summary above the legal text: e-signature counts like ink; paper remains possible.

Identity.consentNext.context: Button advancing from consent to the drawing step.

Identity.drawIntro.source: This is the signature that appears on the reimbursement forms — sign the way you would on paper, with your finger or the mouse.
Identity.drawIntro.context: Instruction above the canvas: this drawing appears on the forms; sign like on paper.

Identity.finishSetup.context: Final wizard button (creates the signing key).

Identity.finishing.context: Busy label for that button. Keep the ellipsis.

Identity.redrawBody.source: Your new signature is used on everything you sign from now on. Nothing you've already signed changes.
Identity.redrawBody.context: Note in that dialog: the new drawing affects future paperwork only.

Identity.saveSignature.context: Save button of the redraw dialog.

Identity.couldNotSave.context: Fallback error for the redraw dialog.

Identity.scopeTitle.context: Heading of the admin's rollout-scope row (allowlist vs everyone), under the master switch.

Identity.scopeAllowlist.context: Scope button: restrict e-signing to the allowlist below. Keep short — segmented button.

Identity.scopeEveryone.context: Scope button: open e-signing to all signed-in users. Keep short — segmented button.

Identity.allowlistOnMembersPage.source: Choose who can use electronic signing on the <link>Members</link> page. Removing someone hides signing from them; anything they already signed stays valid.
Identity.allowlistOnMembersPage.context: Note in the rollout controls pointing at the Members page; <link> wraps the page name. The second sentence is the retention promise.

Identity.scanFromVouchTab.source: Important: they should scan this from the Vouch tab inside this app — not with their phone’s camera app.
Identity.scanFromVouchTab.context: Warning under the candidate's vouch QR: the voucher must scan from the app's Vouch tab, not the phone camera app (which opens a logged-out browser).

Identity.whoCanVouch.source: Members who can vouch for you: {names}{more, plural, =0 {} other { and # more}}.
Identity.whoCanVouch.context: List under the pending-vouch QR naming attested members who can vouch; {names} = comma-joined names, {more} = count beyond the first 8.

Identity.revokedBody.source: Your signing key was retired by an administrator. Everything you signed before stays valid, but to sign again you’ll need to set up signing afresh and be vouched in person again.
Identity.revokedBody.context: Explanation on the identity card when the member's signing key was revoked; pairs with the set-up-again button.

Identity.reEnrollButton.context: Button re-starting enrollment after a key revocation. Short.

Identity.connectFraming.source: First, sign in with the same Google account you use for this app.
Identity.connectFraming.context: One plain line above the connect (Google popup) card on the profile signing card — sets the expectation the nudges promised.

Devices.newDeviceTitle.source: This looks like a new device.
Devices.newDeviceTitle.context: Amber card title when a signed-in member opens the app on a browser that doesn't hold their keys.

Devices.newDeviceBody.source: Your signing identity exists, but it isn't on this device yet. Bring it over — no re-vouching needed.
Devices.newDeviceBody.context: Body of that card (their fleet still exists): bring the identity over; no re-vouching needed.

Devices.newDeviceBodyGone.source: Your signing identity exists, but it isn't on this account anymore. Bring it over — no re-vouching needed unless you start over.
Devices.newDeviceBodyGone.context: Variant when the whole fleet was wiped (post start-over): only starting over remains, unless they start over.

Devices.approveFromOther.source: 📱 Approve from a device you already sign with
Devices.approveFromOther.context: Primary recovery path button: approve this browser from a device that already signs. Keep the 📱.

Devices.typePhraseOption.context: Secondary path button: type the 24-word recovery phrase. Keep the 🔑.

Devices.keysRemovedEverywhere.source: Your signing keys were removed everywhere, so the only way forward is to start over below.
Devices.keysRemovedEverywhere.context: Shown instead of the two buttons when no device holds keys anymore.

Devices.noneWork.context: Collapsed <details> summary of the last-resort help.

Devices.noneWorkBody.source: If your old devices are gone and you never printed a recovery sheet, your identity can't be recovered — that's what keeps it yours. Starting over creates a brand-new signing key: the same people vouch for you again, in person, and the moment they do, your old key stops counting on its own. Worried someone might use the old key before then? Tell your administrator and they can retire it right away.
Devices.noneWorkBody.context: Last-resort explanation: unrecoverable by design; starting over re-vouches and auto-retires the old key; tell the admin only if someone might USE the old key.

Devices.startOver.context: Deliberately buried destructive button: discard the identity and enroll fresh.

Devices.startOverConfirm.source:
Start over from nothing?

This signs ALL your devices out and throws away your current signing key. You'll set up again from scratch and be vouched for again in person — which automatically retires the old key. Anything you already signed stays valid.
Devices.startOverConfirm.context: in-app confirmation dialog for start-over. Keep the blank line (\n\n). Serious but calm: old signatures stay valid.

Devices.startOverConfirmButton.context: Short destructive button in the start-over confirmation dialog, paired with Common.cancel; a verb phrase, matching Devices.startOver's term.

Devices.couldNotStart.context: Error when the device-approval request can't be created.

Devices.waitingIntro.source: Now pick up the device you already sign with. A message appears there — type this code into it:
Devices.waitingIntro.context: Instruction above the big 6-digit code: pick up the other device and type this code there.

Devices.waitingNote.source: Waiting for the other device… this page continues by itself once it's approved.
Devices.waitingNote.context: Small print while waiting; <em> wraps 'this'. Explains the code binds the approval to this browser.

Devices.phrasePlaceholder.context: Textarea placeholder — an example of the phrase format. Recovery phrases are always English BIP39 words: keep the example words in English.

Devices.phraseWrong.source: That phrase didn't work — check the words and their order
Devices.phraseWrong.context: Error when the typed phrase doesn't unlock: check words and their order.

Devices.unlock.context: Submit button of the phrase form.

Devices.deviceWants.context: Banner headline when a new device asks to be approved; {name} is the device's self-reported name, already in quotes.

Devices.approveHint.source: Only approve if it's yours and in your hands. Type the 6-digit code from its screen — that's what proves it's really that device.
Devices.approveHint.context: Banner small print: only approve your own device in your hands; type the code from ITS screen (<em> wraps 'its').

Devices.approve.context: Approve button of the device banner.

Devices.rejectDevice.context: Reject button of the device banner.

Devices.approvalFailed.context: Error when approving the device fails (usually a wrong code).

Devices.devicesTitle.source: Devices that can sign as you
Devices.devicesTitle.context: Small header of the authorized-devices list on the profile card.

Devices.thisDevice.context: Green chip marking the browser you're reading this on.

Devices.addedOn.context: Small suffix per device; {date} is a formatted date. Lowercase fragment.

Devices.removeDevice.context: Small lowercase button removing a device from the list.

Devices.removeConfirm.source:
Sign “{name}” out of electronic signing?

It loses access right away. If the device might be in someone else's hands, tell your administrator in person too, so they can revoke your signing key.
Devices.removeConfirm.context: in-app confirmation dialog for removing a device; {name} is the device name. Keep the blank line (\n\n).

Devices.removeConfirmButton.context: Short destructive button in the device sign-out confirmation dialog, paired with Common.cancel; a verb (sign the device out), matching the wording of Devices.removeConfirm.

Devices.lostHint.source: Lost one? Remove it here, then tell your administrator in person if someone else might have it — they can revoke the signing key itself.
Devices.lostHint.context: Small print under the device list: remove lost devices; tell the admin in person if someone else might have it.

Devices.recoveryRoot.source: Protect the church's signing anchor. If this device is lost with no recovery sheet, electronic signing has to be rebuilt from scratch for everyone.
Devices.recoveryRoot.context: Sticky amber nudge for the ADMIN: the root key anchors everyone — print the recovery sheet.

Devices.recoveryNudge.source: One more safety net: print a recovery sheet and you can sign from a new device even if this one is lost. Without one, losing your devices means starting over and being vouched in person again.
Devices.recoveryNudge.context: Dismissible amber nudge for members: print a recovery sheet as a safety net.

Devices.preparing.context: Placeholder while the phrase is generated. Keep the ellipsis.

Devices.sheetBody.source: This one-page sheet holds the 24 words that can bring your signing identity to a new device. Print it and keep it with your important papers at home. Anyone holding the sheet can sign as you — and nobody, including the church, can recreate it if it's lost.
Devices.sheetBody.context: Explains the sheet: 24 words, keep at home with important papers; anyone holding it can sign as you; nobody can recreate it.

Devices.downloadSheet.context: Download button for the printable PDF. Keep the 🖨️.

Devices.downloadedPrint.context: Same button after downloading: now print it. Keep the ✓.

Devices.noPrinter.source: No printer? Copy the words by hand

Devices.savedCheckbox.source: The sheet is printed (or written out) and stored somewhere safe.
Devices.savedCheckbox.context: Checkbox the member must tick before closing: the sheet is printed/written and stored safely.

Devices.done.context: Final button of the recovery dialog.

Devices.couldNotBuildSheet.context: Error when building the PDF fails.

Devices.waitingCancel.context: Link under the 6-digit device-approval code aborting the wait and returning to the options. Short.

Devices.rejectFailed.source: Could not reject the request — it may still be pending. Try again.
Devices.rejectFailed.context: Error when rejecting a device request fails — the request may still be pending.

Devices.freshPhraseNote.source: Opening this creates a brand-new phrase — any sheet you printed earlier stops working.
Devices.freshPhraseNote.context: Amber warning in the recovery-phrase dialog: each open mints a new phrase, old printed sheets stop working.

Vouch.title.context: Page title of the in-person vouching screen. Must read the same as the 🤝 Vouch for a member link (without the emoji).

Vouch.onlyAttested.source: Only attested members can vouch. Enable signing on your <link>profile</link> and get vouched first.
Vouch.onlyAttested.context: Shown when the visitor can't vouch yet; <link> wraps the word for the profile page.

Vouch.doneTitle.context: Success line after signing a vouch; {name} is the candidate.

Vouch.doneBody.source: They need two member vouches (or one from an approver) to become attested.
Vouch.doneBody.context: Reminder of the attestation rule: two member vouches, or one from an approver.

Vouch.beforeTitle.source: Before you vouch, confirm in person:
Vouch.beforeTitle.context: Bold header of the amber in-person checklist.

Vouch.bullet1.source: The person is physically with you right now.
Vouch.bullet1.context: Checklist: the person is physically present.

Vouch.bullet2.source: This name and email really belong to them.
Vouch.bullet2.context: Checklist: the name and email really belong to them.

Vouch.bullet3.source: You scanned the QR from THEIR screen (not a forwarded link).
Vouch.bullet3.context: Checklist: the QR was scanned from THEIR screen, not a forwarded link.

Vouch.rekeyTitle.context: Warning when the vouch would replace an existing key; {name} is the candidate.

Vouch.rekeyBody.source: Usually that means a lost device. The moment they're vouched back in, the old key stops counting — everything they signed before stays valid. Be extra sure it's really them.
Vouch.rekeyBody.context: Explains re-keying: usually a lost device; old key stops counting once re-vouched; old signatures stay valid; be extra sure.

Vouch.manualLabel.source: Type their full key fingerprint (from their screen — the spoken 6-digit code is never enough):
Vouch.manualLabel.context: Label of the manual fingerprint field (no-QR fallback). The spoken 6-digit code is never sufficient.

Vouch.manualPlaceholder.context: Placeholder describing the expected input: 64 hex characters, at least 32 accepted.

Vouch.fpMismatch.context: Inline error while the typed fingerprint doesn't match.

Vouch.confirmLabel.source: I am with {name} in person and confirm this identity is theirs.
Vouch.confirmLabel.context: The vouch checkbox — a first-person statement of in-person identity confirmation. <b> wraps {name}.

Vouch.scanIntro.source: Scan the candidate's QR right here with your camera. Can't use the camera? Pick them from the list and type their full key fingerprint (read from their screen; the short spoken code is not enough).
Vouch.scanIntro.context: Intro on the vouch page without a QR parameter: scan with the phone camera, or pick a candidate and type the full fingerprint (<b> wraps it).

Vouch.orPick.source: Or pick them and type their fingerprint

Vouch.nobodyPending.context: Empty state of the awaiting-vouches list.

Vouch.vouchFailed.context: Fallback error when signing the vouch fails.

Vouch.malformedLink.source: This vouch link is malformed — rescan the QR
Vouch.malformedLink.context: Error when the scanned vouch URL is corrupt: rescan the QR.

Vouch.membersPageHint.source: Looking for the member roster? Roles, signing keys, and e-sign access are managed on the <link>Members</link> page.
Vouch.membersPageHint.context: Pointer for treasurers/admins at the bottom of the vouch page; <link> wraps the Members page name.

Vouch.keylessDevice.source: This browser doesn’t hold your signing key yet, so vouching from here will fail. Add this device from your Profile first, or vouch from a device you already sign on.
Vouch.keylessDevice.context: Amber warning on the Vouch screen when this browser has no signing key (new device): vouching would fail at the last step; points to adding the device via Profile.

VouchScan.cancel.context: Button that stops the QR-scanner camera during a vouch. Imperative.

VouchScan.aim.source: Point at the candidate's QR code

VouchScan.notVouch.source: That's not a vouch code — line up the candidate's QR code.

VouchScan.insecure.source: This device can't open the camera here. Use the list below instead.

VouchScan.denied.source: Camera access was blocked. Allow the camera for this app in your device settings, or use the list below.

VouchScan.startFailed.source: Couldn't start the camera. Use the list below instead.

Verify.verifying.context: Loading line while signatures are re-checked in the visitor's browser. Keep the ellipsis.

Verify.failedTitle.context: Red card title when verification can't complete.

Verify.linkInvalid.source: This verification link is not valid or has expired
Verify.linkInvalid.context: Error for a bad/expired verification link; {part} is a technical endpoint name (leave as-is).

Verify.confirmedTitle.context: Big verdict headline on the public /v verification page when every check passes. Plain, reassuring — not technical. Pairs with 'warningsTitle'.

Verify.warningsTitle.context: Amber card title when checks finished but something is off.

Verify.storyGeneric.source: This paperwork was cryptographically checked in your browser — see the details below.
Verify.storyGeneric.context: One-line summary under the verdict when the claim's owner/amount are unknown (e.g. a deleted claim). Plain language.

Verify.story.paid.source: {name} asked to be paid back {amount}. A different, authorized person approved it, and the church treasurer paid it — and the paperwork you're looking at hasn't changed since it was signed.
Verify.story.paid.context: Verdict summary sentence for a fully-paid claim. {name}=claimant, {amount}=money; <b> bolds them. The reader scanned a QR and wants the gist.

Verify.story.approved.source: {name} asked to be paid back {amount}. A different, authorized person approved it. The treasurer hasn't recorded payment yet, and the paperwork hasn't changed since it was signed.
Verify.story.approved.context: Verdict summary sentence when the claim is approved but not yet paid. {name}=claimant, {amount}=money.

Verify.story.submitted.source: {name} asked to be paid back {amount} and signed the request. It's waiting for an authorized approver — and nothing has changed since it was signed.
Verify.story.submitted.context: Verdict summary sentence when the claim is submitted and awaiting an approver. {name}=claimant, {amount}=money.

Verify.story.rejected.source: {name} asked to be paid back {amount}, and an authorized approver reviewed it and asked for changes. The paperwork itself is genuine and unchanged.
Verify.story.rejected.context: Verdict summary sentence when an approver sent the claim back for changes. {name}=claimant, {amount}=money.

Verify.story.warn.source: Most of this checks out, but one thing doesn't add up — check the details below before treating this as the genuine paperwork.
Verify.story.warn.context: Verdict summary sentence when a check FAILED — cautionary; tells the reader not to trust the paperwork yet.

Verify.checkedNow.source: Checked on your device just now — not taken on trust from a server.
Verify.checkedNow.context: End of the summary sentence: the verdict was computed just now, in this browser (not by the server).

Verify.pillsLabel.context: Accessibility label (screen readers) for the four-pill lifecycle row. Not visible on screen.

Verify.pillRequested.context: Status pill in a 4-pill row (Requested·Approved·Paid·Unchanged): the reimbursement was requested/submitted by the claimant. One word, past-tense state.

Verify.pillApproved.context: Status pill: an approver signed off. One word, past-tense state — same idea as the claim-status word 'Approved'.

Verify.pillPaid.context: Status pill: the treasurer recorded payment. One word, past-tense state.

Verify.pillUnchanged.context: Status pill: the document has NOT been altered since it was signed. One word; opposite of 'changed/tampered'.

Verify.peopleTitle.context: Small uppercase section heading above the list of people who signed the claim.

Verify.peopleHint.source: Tap anyone to see who confirmed their identity, in person — then keep opening to follow the chain up to the church's root. Links deeper than the direct vouchers are shown by their key, not their name.
Verify.peopleHint.context: Helper sentence under the people heading: tap a person to walk the chain of trust up to the church root; deeper links show a key instead of a name (privacy).

Verify.tag.requested.context: Tiny role tag after a signer's name in the people list: this person requested the money. Lowercase verb phrase. Pairs with tag.approved / tag.paid.

Verify.tag.approved.context: Tiny role tag after a signer's name: this person approved the claim. Lowercase verb phrase.

Verify.tag.paid.context: Tiny role tag after a signer's name: this person (the treasurer) paid the claim. Lowercase verb phrase.

Verify.confirmedInPerson.context: Green badge under a signer's name: their identity was vouched for face-to-face. Short.

Verify.confirmedByLabel.context: Tiny uppercase label above the voucher(s) when a chain-of-trust node is expanded — reads 'Confirmed in person by [the people listed below]'.

Verify.personByKey.context: Placeholder shown INSTEAD of a real name for a voucher deeper in the chain than the direct vouchers (privacy — identified only by the key fingerprint shown beside it). Means 'an unnamed person', not 'person' in general.

Verify.rootName.context: Label for the trust-anchor node at the top of every chain of trust — the church's root identity, shown generically (not the root person's real name) with its key fingerprint beside it. 'root' = the single trust anchor.

Verify.rootCaption.source: The trust anchor set when the church first opened its records. You confirm this one identity out-of-band — the key shown here matches the fingerprint printed in the church's records.
Verify.rootCaption.context: Explanation under the root node: it's the out-of-band trust anchor; the shown key matches the fingerprint the church publishes.

Verify.docTitle.context: Small section heading above the document card. 'what you're holding' = the printed pages the reader has in hand.

Verify.docLeadOk.source: These pages are the approved copy.
Verify.docLeadOk.context: Bold lead line of the document card when checks pass: these pages are the final approved copy.

Verify.docBodyOk.source: The final, stamped version — safe to print and file. Your phone confirmed it's built from the original everyone signed, unchanged.
Verify.docBodyOk.context: Body under docLeadOk: the approved copy is safe to print/file and was confirmed built from the signed original.

Verify.docLeadWarn.source: This copy no longer matches what was signed.
Verify.docLeadWarn.context: Bold lead line of the document card when a check FAILED: the copy no longer matches what was signed.

Verify.docBodyWarn.source: Your phone found that these pages were changed after they were signed. Don't treat them as the genuine paperwork.
Verify.docBodyWarn.context: Body under docLeadWarn: the pages were changed after signing; don't trust them.

Verify.docOpen.context: Button/link that opens the approved-copy PDF. 'copy' = the stamped delivery version.

Verify.stepsTitle.source: Step by step — and what each step sealed
Verify.stepsTitle.context: Small section heading above the step-by-step timeline; each step also shows which document version it 'sealed' (froze).

Verify.submitted.context: Signature line title: the requester's submission signature.

Verify.approved.context: Signature line title: the approval signature.

Verify.rejected.context: Signature line title: the rejection signature.

Verify.paid.context: Signature line title: the payment signature.

Verify.stepSubmit.source: The form was filled out and signed
Verify.stepSubmit.context: Timeline step title for submission: the claimant filled out and signed the form. Shown before the status word 'Submitted'.

Verify.stepApprove.context: Timeline step title for approval: the approver reviewed and approved. Shown before the status word 'Approved'.

Verify.stepReject.context: Timeline step title for rejection: the approver reviewed and declined. Shown before the status word 'Rejected'.

Verify.stepPaid.context: Timeline step title for payment: the treasurer recorded payment. Shown before the status word 'Paid'.

Verify.howSubmit.source: Made with {name}'s own signing key — the identity vouched for above, kept on their device — so this is their signature, not just a typed name.
Verify.howSubmit.context: Plain 'how we know' line under the submit step. {name}=claimant. The signature was made with the person's own signing key, not just a typed name. Avoid overclaiming ('impossible to forge').

Verify.howApprove.source: They reviewed the very same document that was signed, and they are authorized to approve.
Verify.howApprove.context: Plain 'how we know' line under the approve step: the approver saw the same document and is authorized to approve.

Verify.howPaid.source: Only the church treasurer can mark a claim paid.
Verify.howPaid.context: Plain 'how we know' line under the paid step: only the treasurer can mark a claim paid.

Verify.howPaidCheck.source: Only the church treasurer can mark a claim paid. Recorded as check #{number}.
Verify.howPaidCheck.context: Like howPaid but includes the recorded cheque number. {number}=cheque number.

Verify.vtagOriginal.context: Tiny tag on the submit step's document chip: this version is the frozen original that every signature points to. Short.

Verify.vtagApproved.context: Tiny tag on the approve step's document chip: this version is the original plus the approver's stamp (the copy you print). Short.

Verify.vtagNone.context: Tiny tag on the paid step's document chip: payment created no new document version. Short.

Verify.vcapOriginal.source: Signing the form sealed the original — the one version every later signature points to. It's frozen here and can never change.
Verify.vcapOriginal.context: Caption on the sealed-original chip; <b> bolds 'the original'. It's frozen and unchangeable.

Verify.vcapApproved.source: Approving sealed a copy: the original plus the approver's stamp, with the original's mark printed on it — so the stamp can't be moved onto another document.
Verify.vcapApproved.context: Caption on the approved-copy chip; <b> bolds 'sealed a copy'. The copy carries the original's mark so the stamp can't be moved.

Verify.vcapNone.source: The payment didn't create another document — it points back to the approved copy above.
Verify.vcapNone.context: Caption on the paid step's chip: no new document; it points back to the approved copy above.

Verify.viewPage.context: Disclosure toggle on a document chip that reveals this exact version's mark (fingerprint) and a link to open it. 'this exact page' stresses the precise archived bytes.

Verify.markLabel.context: Label before a short hex fingerprint identifying a document version. 'mark' = the version's unique cryptographic fingerprint, NOT a checkmark or symbol.

Verify.openThisPage.context: Link that opens this exact archived document version (a PDF). 'sealed page' = the frozen version.

Verify.lockLine.source: Each step seals its own copy the moment it happens, and every later copy is built from the original and carries its mark. So no page, and no step, can be edited, removed, or swapped afterward.
Verify.lockLine.context: Reassurance line under the timeline: each step seals its own copy and later copies descend from the original, so nothing can be edited, removed, or swapped afterward.

Verify.forAuditors.context: Heading of the collapsed technical/audit disclosure at the bottom. 'auditors' = people doing a technical check.

Verify.rootPinnedCfg.context: Audit check label: the root key matched the deployment's pinned fingerprint.

Verify.rootRelayed.source: root relayed by this server (verify the fingerprint in person)
Verify.rootRelayed.context: Audit check label when no pin is configured: the server relayed the root — verify the fingerprint in person.

Verify.packetMatch.source: packet bytes match the signed hash ({hash}…)
Verify.packetMatch.context: Audit check label: the PDF bytes hash to the signed value; {hash} is a hash prefix.

Verify.packetMismatch.source: packet bytes do NOT match the signed hash
Verify.packetMismatch.context: Audit check label when the bytes do NOT match. Alarming.

Verify.noAnomalies.context: Audit check label: the ledger contains no invalid events.

Verify.anomalyCount.source: {count, plural, one {# ledger anomaly} other {# ledger anomalies}}
Verify.anomalyCount.context: Audit check label with {count} plural invalid events.

Verify.rosterOddities.source: {count, plural, one {# roster oddity} other {# roster oddities}}
Verify.rosterOddities.context: Audit check label with {count} plural roster irregularities.

Verify.invalidEvents.context: Header of the invalid-events list (kept visible on purpose).

Verify.independentCheckCert.source: Independent check: run scripts/verify-bundle.mjs against the certificate's embedded bundle with the church's published root fingerprint.
Verify.independentCheckCert.context: Note for technical auditors; keep the script name in <code> untranslated.

Verify.unknown.context: Fallback signer name when the key maps to nobody on the roster.

Verify.orgHeader.context: Small header above the public verification verdict naming the organization; 'CFCC' is never translated.

Errors.notSignedIn.context: 401 body when the session is missing/expired.

Errors.idTokenRequired.context: 'idToken' is an API field name — keep it verbatim.

Errors.verifiedEmailRequired.source: A verified email address is required

Errors.emailRequired.context: Dev-login validation; lowercase 'email' mirrors the API field name.

Errors.notFound.context: Deliberately vague 404 — used where existence must not leak (capability URLs, cross-tenant). Keep it generic.

Errors.receiptNotFound.context: API error message: the requested receipt doesn't exist (or isn't the user's).

Errors.invalidReceiptUpdate.context: API error message: a receipt-edit request was malformed.

Errors.receiptOnClaim.source: Receipt is part of a claim and cannot be deleted

Errors.noFilesUploaded.context: API error message: an upload request contained no files.

Errors.unsupportedFileType.context: {type} is a raw MIME string like image/heic — keep the placeholder as-is.

Errors.previewPdfOnly.source: Preview is only available for PDF receipts

Errors.invalidPreviewPage.context: API error message: the requested preview page number is out of range/invalid.

Errors.invalidImageEdit.context: API error message: an image-edit request was malformed.

Errors.nothingToChange.context: API error message: an edit request contained no actual changes.

Errors.imageEditImagesOnly.source: Only image receipts can be rotated or cropped

Errors.receiptFrozenByClaim.source: This receipt is on a generated claim — revert that claim before editing the image
Errors.receiptFrozenByClaim.context: 409 for image edits while a generated claim holds the receipt.

Errors.nothingToRestore.source: This receipt has no earlier version to restore

Errors.cropTooSmall.source: Crop region is too small to stay legible
Errors.cropTooSmall.context: Rejects a crop too small to stay legible.

Errors.claimFrozen.source: Claim already generated; line items are frozen
Errors.claimFrozen.context: 409 for row edits on a generated claim; 'frozen' = locked until reverted to draft.

Errors.ministryRequiredToVerify.source: Choose a ministry before verifying this row

Errors.invalidSplitRequest.context: API error message: a line-item split request was malformed.

Errors.splitAmountsInvalid.source: Split amounts must be non-zero and sum to the original amount

Errors.nothingToMergeInto.source: No row above from the same receipt to merge into

Errors.restoreBeforeMerge.source: Restore the excluded row before merging

Errors.claimNotFound.context: API error message: the reimbursement claim doesn't exist (or isn't the user's).

Errors.invalidClaimUpdate.context: API error message: a claim-update request was malformed.

Errors.claimSettingsFrozen.source: Claim already generated; review settings are frozen

Errors.onlyDraftDeletable.source: Only draft claims can be deleted

Errors.receiptsNotFound.source: One or more receipts were not found

Errors.claimReceiptsFrozen.source: Claim already generated; receipts are frozen

Errors.receiptsAlreadyOnClaim.source: One or more receipts are already on this claim

Errors.claimEmpty.source: Claim has no line items to reimburse

Errors.rowsUnverified.source: {count} row(s) still need verification before the PDF can be generated
Errors.rowsUnverified.context: PDF gate message; {count} rows lack verification. English 'row(s)' is a pseudo-plural — Chinese needs none.

Errors.rowsMissingMinistry.source: {count} row(s) still need a ministry before the PDF can be generated
Errors.rowsMissingMinistry.context: PDF gate message; {count} rows lack a ministry.

Errors.profileIncomplete.source: Add your name and mailing address to your profile first — they are printed on the reimbursement form
Errors.profileIncomplete.context: API error when generating the claim PDF while the profile lacks a name/mailing address (both print on the official form).

Errors.invalidManualEntry.context: API error message: manual line-item entry fields were invalid.

Errors.receiptNotOnClaim.source: Receipt not found in this claim

Errors.rowAlreadyEdited.source: This receipt's row has already been edited — adjust it directly

Errors.lastReceiptOnClaim.source: This is the only receipt in the claim — discard the claim instead

Errors.onlyGeneratedRevertible.source: Only generated or under-signature claims can be reverted to draft
Errors.onlyGeneratedRevertible.context: 409 error. “Generated” and “under signature” are claim statuses — match the Common.status wording. Under-signature means submitted/rejected/approved (not yet paid).

Errors.descriptionRequired.source: Describe the claim in a sentence first

Errors.aiRateLimited.source: The AI provider is rate-limited right now — try again in a minute or pick a ministry manually

Errors.aiNoSuggestion.source: The AI couldn't produce a suggestion — pick a ministry manually

Errors.aiQuotaExhausted.source: The AI provider is busy right now — please wait a minute and try again. Affected: {names}
Errors.aiQuotaExhausted.context: 429 after retries; {rpm} requests/minute target, {names} is a list of receipt file names.

Errors.userNotFound.context: API error message: the user record was not found.

Errors.invalidProfileUpdate.context: API error message: a profile-update request was malformed.

Errors.quotaWait.source: Rate limit reached — waiting {seconds}s before retrying ({attempt}/{maxRetries})…
Errors.quotaWait.context: Live status during a rate-limit wait; {seconds} countdown, ({attempt}/{maxRetries}) retry counter — keep the shape.

Errors.pdfFrozen.source: The packet is frozen under signature — download it from the claim page, or revert to draft to edit
Errors.pdfFrozen.context: 409 when regenerating a PDF that is under signature: download the archived packet or revert to draft.

Errors.esign.pickApprover.source: Pick an approver and type your name
Errors.esign.pickApprover.context: 400 in the submit ceremony: approver and typed name are required.

Errors.esign.placeSignature.source: Place your signature on the form first
Errors.esign.placeSignature.context: 400 in the submit ceremony: the drawn signature must be placed on the form first.

Errors.esign.typeNameApproval.source: Type your name to sign an approval
Errors.esign.typeNameApproval.context: 400: the approver's typed-name field is empty.

Errors.esign.selfApprove.source: You cannot approve your own claim
Errors.esign.selfApprove.context: 409: a requester picked themselves as approver.

Errors.esign.notApprover.source: That member is not an attested approver
Errors.esign.notApprover.context: 409: the chosen member isn't an attested approver.

Errors.esign.packetChanged.source: The packet changed since preflight — restart the ceremony
Errors.esign.packetChanged.context: 409: the PDF changed between preflight and signing — restart the ceremony.

Errors.esign.generateFirst.source: Generate the packet PDF before submitting
Errors.esign.generateFirst.context: 409: the claim has no generated PDF yet.

Errors.esign.mirrorBehind.source: Ledger mirror is behind — reconcile this claim first
Errors.esign.mirrorBehind.context: 409: the server's mirror lags the signature ledger; reconcile first ('reconcile' is a background sync the UI performs).

Errors.esign.noPacket.source: No packet for this claim yet
Errors.esign.noPacket.context: 404: no PDF packet exists for the claim yet.

Errors.esign.certNotReady.source: Certificates exist once a claim is approved
Errors.esign.certNotReady.context: 409: certificates only exist once a claim is approved.

Errors.esign.notEnrolled.context: 404: the member has no signing identity yet.

Errors.esign.notSetUp.source: E-sign is not set up yet
Errors.esign.notSetUp.context: 404: the church hasn't set up e-signing.

Errors.esign.switchedOff.context: 409 from every e-sign route while the admin master switch is off.

Errors.esign.notAllowed.source: Electronic signing is not enabled for your account
Errors.esign.notAllowed.context: 409 from e-sign routes when the rollout scope is allowlist and this member is not on it.

Errors.esign.approverUnavailable.source: That approver isn't taking new approval requests right now — pick someone else
Errors.esign.approverUnavailable.context: Error when submitting a claim to an approver who has paused new approval requests (e.g. the picker was stale).

Errors.esign.approverRoleLost.source: Your approver role has been removed — you can decline, but not approve
Errors.esign.approverRoleLost.context: Error when a demoted approver tries to approve a claim still assigned to them; rejecting remains possible.

Errors.esign.decisionNotBinding.source: The signed decision doesn't bind on the ledger — reload this claim and try again, or ask its owner to reassign it
Errors.esign.decisionNotBinding.context: Error when a signed approve/reject reached the signature ledger but does not bind there (e.g. the approver's role was revoked mid-ceremony). “ledger” = the tamper-evident signature record, 账本/帳本.

Errors.esign.invalidNudge.context: Generic failure for the self-serve nudge-preference endpoint (dismissals/snoozes). Low-stakes; a retry happens naturally.

Errors.admin.contextTooLong.source: The church context is too long (max {max} bytes).

Errors.admin.unknownField.source: That setting can’t be edited here.

Errors.admin.invalidValidation.context: API error: the wizard test request was malformed.

Errors.admin.unknownService.context: API error: asked to test a service that has no wizard.

Errors.ministry.invalid.source: Those budget categories couldn’t be saved — check the form and try again.

Errors.ministry.codeFormat.source: Account code must be exactly 3 digits and not 999: “{code}”.

Errors.ministry.codeDuplicate.source: Two active categories share the code {code} — codes must be unique.

Errors.embeddingProbeFailed.source: Could not reach the embedding server. Check the address and key, or use “Save without testing”.

Errors.position.invalid.source: Those positions couldn’t be saved — check the form and try again.

Errors.position.unknownHolder.source: One of the selected people couldn’t be found. Refresh and try again.

Admin.fields_AI_PROVIDER_help.source: Which service runs receipt extraction and the “Suggest ministry” calls. Switch anytime — it applies to the next claim built or suggestion; past extractions and their logs are untouched. Fill in the matching API key below.

Admin.fields_OPENROUTER_API_KEY_help.source: Your OpenRouter key, used only while the provider above is OpenRouter. Stored on the server and never shown again — leave blank to keep the current one.

Admin.fields_OPENROUTER_MODEL_help.source: The OpenRouter model id used for extraction and suggestions when OpenRouter is the provider. Blank uses the default, google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite.

Admin.fields_GEMINI_API_KEY_help.source: Your Google AI Studio (Gemini) key, used only while the provider above is Google. Stored on the server and never shown again — leave blank to keep the current one.

Admin.fields_GEMINI_MODEL_help.source: The Gemini model id used when Google is the provider. Blank uses the default, gemini-3.1-flash-lite.

Admin.fields_AI_RPM_TARGET_help.source: Caps how many provider calls per minute the server makes, so a batch of receipts stays under your plan’s limit (Gemini’s free tier is 15/min). Extra calls wait their turn instead of failing. No effect while AI is mocked. Default 15.

Admin.fields_AI_QUOTA_COOLDOWN_MS_help.source: After a rate-limit error, how long to wait before retrying, in milliseconds. The wait is shown to whoever is building the claim. Default 60000 (one minute).

Admin.fields_AI_QUOTA_MAX_RETRIES_help.source: How many times to retry after a rate-limit error before giving up (0 surfaces it at once). Applies to receipt extraction; the interactive Suggest call never waits. Default 3.

Admin.fields_EXTRACTION_PACE_MS_label.context: Admin settings field label for EXTRACTION_PACE_MS, shown with the raw env-var name beside it. 'reading' = the AI reading/transcribing receipt photos in the background.

Admin.fields_EXTRACTION_PACE_MS_help.source: Minimum gap between the background receipt reader's AI calls, in milliseconds. It reads newly uploaded receipts on its own so claims are instant later; keep it slow to leave your per-minute AI budget for people actively filing. 0 removes the gap. Default 60000 (one receipt a minute).
Admin.fields_EXTRACTION_PACE_MS_help.context: Help text under the EXTRACTION_PACE_MS admin field. Keep '60000' and '0' literal; 'claims' = reimbursement claims (报销单).

Admin.fields_PUBLIC_BASE_URL_help.source: This deployment’s public web address. When set, generated PDFs carry a QR code back to the claim packet and signature certificates link to it. A reverse proxy hides the real URL, so it’s explicit here. Changing it only affects PDFs generated afterward; it’s also required for first-party sign-in below.

Admin.fields_ESIGN_ROOT_EMAIL_help.source: The one account allowed to run the one-time e-signature setup. This matters only BEFORE setup: once e-signatures are established the root identity is fixed in the signed genesis record, so changing this afterward does nothing (re-rooting is a separate re-genesis ceremony, not an email change).

Admin.fields_ESIGN_ROOT_FINGERPRINT_help.source: Optional out-of-band fingerprint of the root signing key. When set, this app and every verifier reject any roster whose root key doesn’t match — the guard against a swapped trust anchor. Set it before setup to pin the expected root; afterward it must equal the established root’s fingerprint or verification fails closed.

Admin.fields_FIREBASE_API_KEY_help.source: From your Firebase web-app config. This is a public client identifier (every browser receives it), shown here so you can verify it. Google sign-in appears only when this, the auth domain, and the project ID are all set.

Admin.fields_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN_help.source: From your Firebase web-app config — usually your-project.firebaseapp.com. The sign-in domain in the normal flow; if you enable first-party sign-in below, point it at your own domain instead. A full https:// URL is accepted (only the host is used).

Admin.fields_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID_help.source: Your Firebase project id. Used both in the browser sign-in config and on the server to verify the Google ID tokens users present — so it must match the project your users actually sign in to. It also derives the first-party sign-in target.

Admin.fields_FIREBASE_APP_ID_help.source: Optional, from the Firebase web-app config. Sign-in works without it; include it only if your setup requires it.

Admin.fields_FIREBASE_AUTH_PROXY_help.source: Serve Google sign-in from this app’s own domain instead of firebaseapp.com — fixes iOS/Safari sign-in failures. Needs the public web address above, plus the redirect URI and authorized domain registered in the Firebase console.

Admin.fields_FIREBASE_AUTH_UPSTREAM_HOST.source: Advanced. The host the first-party sign-in proxy forwards to. Blank derives it from the project id (project.firebaseapp.com), which is right for normal projects — set this only for unusual Firebase setups.

Admin.fields_ADMIN_EMAILS_help.source: Emails that get admin access to this area, comma-separated — the way to add admins without the e-sign roster. It’s app-access only and never changes anyone’s signing role. Takes effect immediately; keep your own email here so you don’t lock yourself out.

Admin.fields_TIME_ZONE_help.context: Help text under the time-zone field in admin deployment settings. “IANA name” is the technical zone-name standard — keep “America/Los_Angeles” verbatim (never translated). Hayward is the church’s city (海沃, see GLOSSARY).
