Admin.wizard.ai.done.source: Receipt reading is configured. Generate a claim to see it in action.

Admin.wizard.ai.steps.provider.intro.source: Choose which backend reads receipts — OpenRouter or Google AI Studio.

Admin.wizard.ai.steps.credentials.intro.source: Enter the key and model for the provider you chose above. The test makes one tiny call to confirm they work.

Admin.wizard.ai.steps.limits.intro.source: Optional. How fast to call the model and how to back off when the provider is busy.

Admin.wizard.firebase.title.context: Setup-wizard title for Google/Firebase sign-in. Short.

Admin.wizard.firebase.blurb.source: Google sign-in — the identity layer the whole app sits on.

Admin.wizard.firebase.done.source: Sign-in is configured. Open the app in a private window to try signing in.

Admin.wizard.firebase.steps.webconfig.in.source: From the Firebase console → Project settings → Your apps. These values are client-safe.

Admin.wizard.firebase.steps.ios.intro.source: Optional but recommended if members use iPhones: serve Firebase's sign-in helper from your own origin so WebKit's storage rules don't break it. Requires your public URL plus two console entries.

Admin.wizard.search.title.context: Setup-wizard title for semantic search. Short.

Admin.wizard.search.blurb.source: Natural-language search over receipts and claims.

Admin.wizard.search.done.source: Search is set up. It indexes existing content in the background; new receipts index within seconds.

Admin.wizard.search.steps.endpoint.intro.source: The address of your embedding server and its model. The test connects and detects the vector size for you.

Admin.wizard.search.steps.enable.intro.source: Enable search and set how close a match has to be to show up. Saving here starts the first index build.

Admin.checks.push.mock.source: Mock mode — deliveries are recorded locally, no real sending.

Admin.checks.push.vapidMissing.source: Web Push key is missing — devices can't be registered without it.

Admin.checks.push.senderMissing.source: Sender ID is missing — add it so the browser can register for push.

Admin.checks.push.saMissing.source: No sending credential yet — paste the service-account JSON.

Admin.checks.push.saInvalid.source: That doesn't look like a service-account JSON key.

Admin.checks.push.saParsed.context: Dry-run result; {email} is the service-account email, verbatim.

Admin.checks.push.scopeOk.source: The service account can send messages and nothing more — exactly right.

Admin.checks.push.scopeBroad.source: This service account can do more than send messages. That weakens the e-sign security model — recreate it with only the custom messaging role.

Admin.checks.push.scopeUnknown.source: Couldn't verify the service account's permissions from here. Double-check it holds only the custom messaging role.

Admin.checks.push.quietValid.source: Quiet hours understood ({count, plural, one {# window} other {# windows}}).

Admin.checks.push.quietInvalid.source: Quiet hours couldn't be read — expected times like 21:30-08:00,sun:09:00-12:30.

Admin.checks.ai.providerSelected.context: Dry-run result fragment; {provider} is a product name (OpenRouter / Google), keep as-is.

Admin.checks.ai.providerInvalid.source: Unknown provider "{provider}" — choose OpenRouter or Google.

Admin.checks.ai.modelPresent.context: Dry-run result fragment; {model} is a model id, keep verbatim.

Admin.checks.ai.mock.source: Mock mode — receipt reading returns fixed sample data, no API calls.

Admin.checks.ai.keyMissing.source: {key} is missing — the model can't be reached without it.

Admin.checks.ai.callOk.source: The model answered in {ms} ms — key and model work.

Admin.checks.firebase.proxyBaseUrl.source: Public URL is set — the iPhone sign-in proxy can use it.

Admin.checks.firebase.proxyNeedsBaseUrl.source: The iPhone sign-in proxy needs your public URL — set it, or turn the proxy off.

Admin.checks.search.mock.source: Mock mode — search uses recorded vectors, no endpoint call.

Admin.searchFields.endpoint.label.context: Field label: the embedding server URL. Short.

Admin.searchFields.endpoint.help.source: Base URL of your embedding server (llama.cpp / OpenAI-compatible).

Admin.searchFields.model.label.context: Field label: the embedding model name. One word.

Admin.searchFields.model.help.source: The embedding model name your server serves.

Admin.searchFields.apiKey.label.context: Field label: API key for the embedding endpoint. Short.

Admin.searchFields.apiKey.help.source: Sent as a Bearer token to the endpoint. Leave blank if it needs none.

Admin.searchFields.enabled.label.context: Toggle label: turn semantic search on. Short.

Admin.searchFields.enabled.help.source: Turn semantic search on and start indexing.

Admin.searchFields.minScore.label.context: Field label: the similarity threshold (0-1). Short.

Admin.searchFields.minScore.help.source: How close a match must be to show up, 0–1 (higher = stricter). 0.25 is a good start.

Ministries.subtitle.source: The list requesters pick from, and the descriptions that guide them. Church-wide.

Ministries.loadDefaults.context: Button in the treasurer's budget-category editor, beside '+ Add group'. Stages the app's built-in category list into the editor (adds missing categories, fills blank descriptions). 'defaults' = the built-in chart of accounts, not a settings reset. Keep short.

Ministries.loadDefaultsTitle.source: Add any missing built-in categories and fill blank descriptions with the built-in guidance. Nothing is overwritten, and nothing is saved until you save.
Ministries.loadDefaultsTitle.context: Tooltip on the 'Load defaults' button explaining what it stages; 'save' refers to the editor's own Save button.

Ministries.defaultsLoaded.source: Loaded the built-in defaults — {added, plural, =0 {no new categories} one {# new category} other {# new categories}} added, {filled, plural, =0 {no blank descriptions} one {# blank description} other {# blank descriptions}} filled in. Review the changes, then save.
Ministries.defaultsLoaded.context: Info banner after clicking 'Load defaults': reports the staged (unsaved) changes. {added} = new categories appended, {filled} = blank description fields filled with built-in guidance.

Ministries.defaultsNoop.source: Nothing to load — every built-in category is already in the list, and no blank description matched one.
Ministries.defaultsNoop.context: Info banner after clicking 'Load defaults' when there was nothing to stage: every built-in category code already exists and no blank description had a built-in match.

Ministries.descriptionPlaceholder.source: Add a hint for requesters — it also guides the AI’s category suggestions…

Ministries.renameWarning.source: Renaming or recoding a category that’s already on claims won’t relabel those past rows — their printed value stays as it was. To retire one, archive it instead.

Ministries.reservedNote.source: Codes are 3 digits. 999 is reserved for uncategorized “Other…” entries and never prints on the form.

Ministries.empty.source: No categories yet — add one to get started.

Ministries.routesNobody.source: No holder here can approve yet — claims won't be pre-filled.

Ministries.routesNoHolder.source: This position has no holder yet.

Ministries.removeUnsavedAria.context: aria-label of the ✕ removing a never-saved category row; {name} = its name/code or the newCategoryFallback.

Ministries.newCategoryFallback.context: Fallback noun used in removeUnsavedAria when a brand-new row has no name yet.

Search.title.context: Page title of the search screen and (via SAME_VALUE_GROUPS) the nav tab + submit button. One word. Use 搜索 (Hans) / 搜尋 (Hant) per glossary.

Search.searchButton.context: The submit button beside the search input. Same word as Search.title (declared same-value).

Search.searching.context: Button/aria label while a search is in flight. Short, ends with ellipsis.

Search.stillSearching.context: Shown when a search exceeds ~3 s (server cold start). Reassurance, not an error.

Search.searchFailed.source: Search didn't go through. Please try again.
Search.searchFailed.context: Generic search failure line, shown above stale results which remain usable.

Search.scopeLabel.context: aria-label of the three-segment scope control (role-holders only).

Search.scopeMine.context: Scope segment 1 of 3: search only the caller's own receipts/claims. Very short — segment control chip.

Search.scopeAll.context: Scope segment 2 of 3: search every member's receipts/claims. 'Whole church' = the congregation's data, use 全教会/全教會 per glossary. Very short.

Search.scopeDecided.context: Scope segment 3 of 3: claims this approver already approved OR rejected (completed aspect — 我审批过的, never 决定; bare 我审批的 would read as 'awaiting my approval'). Very short.

Search.scopeDecidedHint.source: Claims you approved or rejected — not ones still waiting on you. Leave the search box empty to list them all.
Search.scopeDecidedHint.context: Helper text under the decided scope, lowercase fragment: clarifies it excludes claims still waiting for a decision.

Search.typeFilterLabel.context: Caption before the 3-way result-type filter (Both / Receipts / Claims); also its aria-label. Reads 'Show: Receipts'. Very short — pairs with 'Where'. Verb 'show/display', not the noun 'performance'.

Search.scopeFilterLabel.context: Caption before the scope control (My items / Whole church / Claims I decided). Reads 'Where: Whole church' — i.e. which pool to search. Very short — pairs with 'Show'. Use 范围/範圍 (scope), not a location word.

Search.typeAll.context: Type filter segment 1 of 3: show both receipts and claims (no type restriction). Very short — segment control chip. 全部 = 'all/both'.

Search.typeReceipts.context: Type filter segment 2 of 3: show only receipts. Very short — segment control chip. Glossary 收据/收據.

Search.typeClaims.context: Type filter segment 3 of 3: show only reimbursement claims. Very short — segment control chip. Glossary 报销单/報銷單.

Search.exactMatches.context: Section header over results that literally contain the typed words (vs the descriptive/semantic results below). ALL-CAPS styling applied by CSS.

Search.showAllExact.context: Button expanding the exact-match strip beyond the first 3. {count} = total exact matches.

Search.bestMatch.context: Section header over the single highest-ranked descriptive result. Only shown when there are no exact matches.

Search.notOnClaim.context: Chip on a receipt result that is not attached to any claim — the cue that it can be claimed now. Short.

Search.findInReceipts.context: Action on an unclaimed receipt result: jumps to the Receipts page with this receipt highlighted. 'Receipts' = the capture page (收据/收據).

Search.onClaim.context: Chip on a receipt result: it is on a claim with the given status. {status} is a translated Common.status value.

Search.receiptFallbackTitle.context: Card title fallback when a receipt has no extracted merchant yet. One word.

Search.recentSearches.context: Label/heading of the recent-searches dropdown that opens under the search box on focus (the user's past queries, synced across their devices).

Search.clearRecents.context: Button in the top-right of the recent-searches dropdown; deletes the user's whole saved search history. 'history' = search history (their past queries), not the browser's back history. Keep it short — sits beside the 'Recent searches' heading.

Search.emptyTitle.context: Empty-state headline when a search found nothing.

Search.emptyBody.source: Try fewer or different words — exact wording was searched too.
Search.emptyBody.context: Empty-state body: suggests rewording; reassures that exact text was already tried.

Search.emptyPendingTitle.context: Empty-state headline when nothing matched BUT the caller's own items are still being indexed.

Search.pendingReceipts.source: {count, plural, one {1 of your receipts is still being added to search — usually ready in about a minute.} other {# of your receipts are still being added to search — usually ready in about a minute.}}
Search.pendingReceipts.context: Freshness note: {count} of the caller's receipts are queued for indexing (~a minute).

Search.pendingClaims.source: {count, plural, one {A claim you edited recently will appear in search a few minutes after you stop editing.} other {# claims you edited recently will appear in search a few minutes after you stop editing.}}
Search.pendingClaims.context: Freshness note for drafts: claims index a few minutes after editing stops (10-min debounce).

Search.pendingRebuild.source: Search is still indexing older items — about {count} to go.
Search.pendingRebuild.context: Freshness note during a full index rebuild; {count} is a rounded global figure.

Search.resultsAnnounce.source: {count, plural, =0 {No results} one {# result} other {# results}}
Search.resultsAnnounce.context: aria-live announcement after a search completes. {count} = total results.

Search.degraded.source: Right now search only finds results containing your exact words. Descriptive search will be back soon.
Search.degraded.context: Banner when the embedding backend is down: only exact-text matching is working. Describe the outcome, not the subsystem.

Search.dateHint.source: Search matches descriptions, not dates — dates are shown on each result.
Search.dateHint.context: Hint when the query contains a relative-date phrase (e.g. 上个月) but search cannot filter by date.

Search.showMore.context: Load-more button for the decided-claims browse (20 per page).

Search.appliedQuery.context: Annotation line above the search results echoing the query they were fetched with; {query} = the user's search text (never translated). Followed inline by small Show/Where filter pills. Short.

Search.appliedAll.context: Same annotation line when the search box was EMPTY (the decided/team scopes list everything) — labels the full listing. Two words.

Search.staleNote.source: Filters changed — search again to update.
Search.staleNote.context: Amber note appended to the annotation line when the query or Show/Where controls no longer match the displayed results; tells the user to press the Search button again. 'Filters' = the Show/Where controls and query.

Search.scopeTeamHint.source: Receipts and claims under your teams' budget categories. Leave the search empty to list all of them.

Search.viewOnlyCard.source: View only — you don’t have access to open this claim.
Search.viewOnlyCard.context: Caption on a search result card the viewer cannot open (team read grant covers the receipt, not the claim page).

Search.emptyDegradedBody.source: Descriptive search is temporarily unavailable and nothing matched the exact wording — try again shortly.
Search.emptyDegradedBody.context: Empty-state body when the embedding backend is down AND nothing matched exactly — must not read as a plain 'no matches'.

Search.emptyTeamBrowse.source: Your teams have no receipts on submitted claims yet — new ones will appear here.
Search.emptyTeamBrowse.context: Empty-state body when browsing the team scope with an empty query and no team receipts exist yet.

Search.unavailableTitle.context: Title of the friendly card shown at /search while the backend is unconfigured. Short.

Search.unavailableBody.source: An administrator needs to configure the search backend before this page works. Everything else in the app is unaffected.
Search.unavailableBody.context: Body of that card: an administrator must configure search; the rest of the app works.

Positions.subtitle.source: Custom approval roles you assign to people and use as the default approver for budget categories.

Positions.authorityNote.source: A position is a routing label, not authority. Assigning someone here does not let them approve — they still need the Approver role, granted through the signature roster on the <link>Members</link> page.

Positions.empty.source: No positions yet — add one to set default approvers for your budget categories.

Positions.loadDefaults.context: Button in the empty Positions editor. Populates the editor with the standard set of deacon positions (approval roles) so the treasurer can assign people and save. Not a destructive reset — it only appears when no positions exist.

Positions.loadDefaultsHint.source: Adds the standard deacon roles, ready for you to assign people and save.
Positions.loadDefaultsHint.context: Helper text under the Load default positions button, explaining what it does.

Positions.builtinBadge.context: Small tag next to a built-in position's name in the Positions editor, marking it a standard role (its name is fixed and can't be renamed).

Positions.builtin.chineseCaring.context: Name of a built-in deacon role (a church leadership position) shown in the Positions editor, approver picker, members directory, and budget-category default-approver list. Authoritative church terminology — preserve the provided translation exactly.

Positions.builtin.chineseEvangelism.context: Name of a built-in deacon role (a church leadership position) shown in the Positions editor, approver picker, members directory, and budget-category default-approver list. Authoritative church terminology — preserve the provided translation exactly.

Positions.builtin.childrensMinistry.context: Name of a built-in deacon role (a church leadership position) shown in the Positions editor, approver picker, members directory, and budget-category default-approver list. Authoritative church terminology — preserve the provided translation exactly.

Positions.builtin.englishDiscipleship.context: Name of a built-in deacon role (a church leadership position) shown in the Positions editor, approver picker, members directory, and budget-category default-approver list. Authoritative church terminology — preserve the provided translation exactly.

Positions.builtin.englishEvangelism.context: Name of a built-in deacon role (a church leadership position) shown in the Positions editor, approver picker, members directory, and budget-category default-approver list. Authoritative church terminology — preserve the provided translation exactly.

Positions.builtin.finance.context: Name of a built-in deacon role (a church leadership position) shown in the Positions editor, approver picker, members directory, and budget-category default-approver list. Authoritative church terminology — preserve the provided translation exactly.

Positions.builtin.generalAffairs.context: Name of a built-in deacon role (a church leadership position) shown in the Positions editor, approver picker, members directory, and budget-category default-approver list. Authoritative church terminology — preserve the provided translation exactly.

Positions.builtin.missions.context: Name of a built-in deacon role (a church leadership position) shown in the Positions editor, approver picker, members directory, and budget-category default-approver list. Authoritative church terminology — preserve the provided translation exactly.

Positions.builtin.property.context: Name of a built-in deacon role (a church leadership position) shown in the Positions editor, approver picker, members directory, and budget-category default-approver list. Authoritative church terminology — preserve the provided translation exactly.

Positions.builtin.worship.context: Name of a built-in deacon role (a church leadership position) shown in the Positions editor, approver picker, members directory, and budget-category default-approver list. Authoritative church terminology — preserve the provided translation exactly.

Positions.nameEnLabel.context: Accessible label for the English name input of a custom position in the Positions editor. English is the required fallback name.

Positions.nameZhHansPlaceholder.context: Placeholder/label for the optional Simplified Chinese name input of a custom position in the Positions editor.

Positions.nameZhHantPlaceholder.context: Placeholder/label for the optional Traditional Chinese name input of a custom position in the Positions editor.

Positions.descriptionPlaceholder.context: Placeholder text in a free-text input on the position editor card, under the position's name — a short note describing the role itself (e.g. its scope or who holds it), not a list of what it approves.

Positions.delete.context: Label for the button that permanently deletes a custom approval role (Position) in the Positions editor; also the confirm button.

Positions.deleteConfirm.source: Delete “{name}”? This permanently removes the position.
Positions.deleteConfirm.context: Confirmation prompt shown before permanently deleting a Position. {name} is the position's name.

Positions.deleteConfirmInUse.source: It is the default approver for {count, plural, one {# budget category} other {# budget categories}} — that default will be cleared.
Positions.deleteConfirmInUse.context: Extra warning in the delete confirmation when the position is set as the default approver for one or more budget categories; deleting clears that default.

Positions.usedByCategories.context: Shown under a position's description on the position editor card when it is the default approver for one or more budget categories. {list} is a pre-formatted, locale-aware conjunction list (e.g. "245 Drinking Water, 101 Missions, and 102 Youth") of budget-category names — data, passed through as-is, never translated.

Positions.usedByNone.source: Not used by any budget category

Positions.holderPausedWarn.source: {name} has paused approvals, so claims won't pre-fill to them until they resume.

Positions.holderCannotWarn.source: {name} can't approve until an executive officer or admin grants them the Approver role on the <link>Members</link> page.

Positions.routingNote.source: A claim that spans several budget categories pre-fills the approver from its largest-dollar category’s default; unavailable holders are skipped.
Positions.routingNote.context: Info line in the Positions editor explaining how multi-category claims pick their pre-filled approver.

Positions.localizedNamesHint.source: Chinese names are optional — the English name is used where one is blank.
Positions.localizedNamesHint.context: Tooltip on the Chinese name inputs: optional, English used when blank.

Members.title.context: Page title of the Members administration page (the church directory: roles, positions, e-sign). Also the account-menu link and the button on the Positions page (same-value group). One word.

Members.subtitle.source: Roles, positions, e-sign enrollment, and access for everyone in the church — managed in one place.
Members.subtitle.context: Subtitle under the Members page title.

Members.loading.context: Placeholder while the member directory loads.

Members.loadFailed.context: Client-side fallback error when the directory fetch fails.

Members.saveFailed.context: Client-side fallback error when the e-sign access toggle fails.

Members.actionFailed.context: Fallback error when a role change or key revocation fails to sign.

Members.esignOffNote.source: Electronic signing is turned off right now, so enrollment status and access controls are hidden here.
Members.esignOffNote.context: Info note on the Members page while the e-sign master switch is off.

Members.othersTitle.context: Header of the not-yet-attested section of the directory. Short.

Members.othersEmpty.source: Everyone in the directory has been attested.
Members.othersEmpty.context: Empty state of the not-yet-attested section: everyone is attested.

Members.statusPending.context: Members-page chip for an enrolled member awaiting the in-person confirmation. Candidate lexicon (当面确认 family) — matches the page's script wording.

Members.statusRevoked.context: Status chip: their signing key was revoked. Short.

Members.statusNone.context: Status chip: never set up e-sign. Short.

Members.accessGrant.context: Button: allow this member to use e-sign (rollout allowlist). Short.

Members.accessCancel.context: Button: take this member's e-sign access away (rollout allowlist; nothing they signed is voided). Short.

Members.roleLabel.context: Label before the role select in the Members page's attested list.

Members.roleDialogTitle.context: Confirm-dialog title for changing a member's role; {name} is the member.

Members.roleDialogFromTo.source: This changes their role from {from} to {to}.
Members.roleDialogFromTo.context: Confirm-dialog line; {from}/{to} are translated role names (Member/Approver/Treasurer). Keep the <b> tags.

Members.roleExplainMember.source: Members can prepare and sign their own reimbursements, but can't approve or pay others'.
Members.roleExplainMember.context: Confirm-dialog blurb: what a plain member can do.

Members.roleExplainApprover.source: Approvers can approve reimbursement claims for payment.
Members.roleExplainApprover.context: Confirm-dialog blurb: what an approver can do.

Members.roleExplainSecretary.source: The secretary is an executive officer of the board: they can approve reimbursement claims and change members' roles.
Members.roleExplainSecretary.context: Confirm-dialog blurb on the Members page: what the secretary role means (executive officer; approver authority plus changing roles — no payment powers).

Members.roleExplainChairman.source: The chairman is an executive officer of the board: they can approve reimbursement claims and change members' roles.
Members.roleExplainChairman.context: Confirm-dialog blurb on the Members page: what the chairman role means (executive officer; approver authority plus changing roles — no payment powers).

Members.roleExplainTreasurer.source: Treasurers can approve claims and mark them paid, and as an executive officer can change members' roles.
Members.roleExplainTreasurer.context: Confirm-dialog blurb: what a treasurer can do (approver powers plus marking paid).

Members.roleDialogConfirm.context: Confirm button on the role-change dialog.

Members.revokeKey.context: Signing-key action button, and the confirm button on its dialog.

Members.keyDialogTitle.context: Confirm-dialog title for revoking a member's signing key; {name} is the member.

Members.keyDialogBody.source: Do this only when a device that could sign as them was lost or stolen. Everything they've already signed stays valid, but the key stops working everywhere — they'll set up signing again and get vouched back in person.
Members.keyDialogBody.context: Confirm-dialog body explaining what revoking the signing key does. Old signatures stay valid.

Members.attestedSince.context: Small line on an attested member row; {date} = when they were vouched in. 'Attested' = fully vouched signer.

Members.statusRevokedHint.source: Their signing key was retired. To sign again they must set up signing afresh on their Profile and be vouched in person.
Members.statusRevokedHint.context: Tooltip on the 'Key revoked' chip: what the member must do to sign again.

Members.statusNoneHint.source: They haven’t set up electronic signing. It starts from their own Profile page.
Members.statusNoneHint.context: Tooltip on the 'No e-sign yet' chip: enrollment starts from their own Profile.

Members.tallySetUp.context: Rollout tally chip: members who completed e-sign setup (attested). Keep to a few words.

Members.tallyNotYet.context: Rollout tally chip: offered e-sign but not set up and not marked prefers-paper. Neutral — NOT a deficiency.

Members.tallyPaper.context: Rollout tally chip: members recorded (after a conversation) as preferring the paper process.

Members.tallyStalePending.context: Rollout tally chip: enrollments stuck awaiting the in-person confirmation for 14+ days — the treasurer's cue to plan a vouching moment.

Members.selfVouchHint.source: You can confirm waiting members yourself — one scan each on the <link>Vouch</link> page.
Members.selfVouchHint.context: Shown to an ATTESTED officer when members are waiting for vouches. Officer-facing, so the 担保/擔保 register is correct here.

Members.selfVouchBlocked.source: Waiting members need an attested voucher — <link>set up signing</link> to confirm them yourself.
Members.selfVouchBlocked.context: Shown instead of selfVouchHint when the viewing officer is NOT attested themselves (role alone cannot vouch).

Members.awaitingDutyHint.source: Confirmed and holds a Position, but the approver role hasn't been granted yet — until it is, claims can't be routed to them. Grant it with the Role control on this row.
Members.awaitingDutyHint.context: Amber note on an attested member's row when they hold a Position but role is still member; names the adjacent Role control as the remedy.

Members.prefersPaperChip.context: Row chip for a member recorded as preferring the paper process.

Members.prefersPaperSet.context: Button: record (after a conversation) that this member prefers paper — silences their setup suggestions.

Members.prefersPaperHint.source: Record this after a real conversation — it stops the app's setup suggestions for this member. Paper is always fine.

Members.helpTitle.context: Summary label of the collapsed in-person-script disclosure on the Members page.

Members.helpScriptPrivacy.source: Some members may have chosen paper in the app — they won't have told you. Ask first: “Want help setting up, or are you set with paper?” and record it here.
Members.helpScriptPrivacy.context: First line of the in-person script: in-app declines are private, so the conversation must ask, never assume.

Members.helpScriptSetup.source: Setup lives on each member's Profile page; the in-person confirmation is one QR scan.
Members.helpScriptSetup.context: Second line of the in-person script.

Members.helpScriptSeniors.source: Seniors may need a hand with the Google sign-in step — plan a minute per person after the service.
Members.helpScriptSeniors.context: Third line of the in-person script.

Members.helpScriptPaper.source: Paper remains completely fine. Early on, every confirmation goes through the few attested members — plan the after-service table accordingly.
Members.helpScriptPaper.context: Fourth line of the in-person script.

Teams.subtitle.source: Groups of members who can view the receipts filed under their budget categories.

Teams.readOnlyNote.source: Team membership is view-only. Members can see, list, and search receipts on filed claims under the team's budget categories — they can't edit anything, and drafts stay private to their owners.

Teams.empty.source: No teams yet — add one to share budget-category receipts with its members.

Teams.noMinistries.source: No budget categories yet — members won't see anything until one is added.

Teams.archiveEndsAccess.source: Archiving ends every member’s read access when you save.
Teams.archiveEndsAccess.context: Amber note under a team's Archived toggle: saving the archive ends members' read access.

Notifications.push.signingRequest.title.context: Push notification title on a lock screen. ACTION VERB FIRST, the {label} (claim event name, user data, verbatim) LAST so tray truncation eats the label, never the verb — keep that order in Chinese too. Use 您-register (formal) for all Notifications.* text.

Notifications.push.signingRequest.titleB.context: Same push title when the claim has no event label — must read as a complete phrase on its own, no dangling dash.

Notifications.push.signingRequest.body.context: Push body under the signing-request title. {name} is the submitter's name, verbatim.

Notifications.push.signingRequest.bodyBa.source: A claim is waiting for your signature.
Notifications.push.signingRequest.bodyBa.context: Push body when the submitter's name is unavailable. Complete sentence.

Notifications.push.claimApproved.title.context: Push title to the claim owner. Action first, {label} last (see signingRequest.title).

Notifications.push.claimApproved.titleBa.context: Approved push title without a label. Complete phrase.

Notifications.push.claimRejected.title.context: Push title to the owner when the approver asked for changes. Deliberately soft ('needs changes', not 'rejected'). Action first, {label} last.

Notifications.push.claimRejected.titleBa.context: Needs-changes push title without a label.

Notifications.push.claimRejected.body.source: Tap to see the reviewer’s note.
Notifications.push.claimRejected.body.context: Push body inviting the tap; the reviewer's note itself NEVER appears on the lock screen.

Notifications.push.financeQueue.title.source: {count, plural, =1 {A claim is ready for payment} other {# claims are ready for payment}}
Notifications.push.financeQueue.title.context: Push title to treasurers; coalesces — ICU plural on {count} (approved claims awaiting payment).

Notifications.push.financeQueue.bodySing.context: Push body when exactly one claim is ready; {label} = claim event name, verbatim. Also reused as the activity-list line for this event.

Notifications.push.claimPaid.title.context: Push title to the owner when a treasurer records payment. No amounts, no check number — deliberate.

Notifications.push.claimPaid.titleBare.context: Paid push title without a label.

Notifications.push.deviceRequest.title.source: A new device asked to join your signing identity
Notifications.push.deviceRequest.title.context: SECURITY push: a new device asked to join the user's e-sign identity. Calm but attention-getting.

Notifications.push.deviceRequest.body.source: If this wasn’t you, review now.
Notifications.push.deviceRequest.body.context: Security push body. 'review now' = open the app and look at the device request banner.

Notifications.push.selfTest.title.context: The test notification a user sends themselves from the profile card.

Notifications.push.selfTest.body.source: Notifications are working on this device.

Notifications.push.discreet.signing.context: Discreet-mode replacement title: reveals NOTHING about who/what on a shared lock screen. Pairs with discreet.body.

Notifications.push.discreet.claims.source: An update on one of your claims
Notifications.push.discreet.claims.context: Discreet-mode replacement title for any own-claim event (approved/changes/paid) — outcome-neutral on purpose.

Notifications.push.discreet.finance.source: Claims may be ready for payment
Notifications.push.discreet.finance.context: Discreet-mode replacement title for the treasurer queue event.

Notifications.push.discreet.body.source: Open Numbers to see the details.

Notifications.card.title.context: Profile page card heading for notification settings. One word.

Notifications.card.undo.source: Turn everything off anytime: Profile → Notifications → Off. This works even if your phone’s settings are wrong.
Notifications.card.undo.context: THE taught undo sentence — opens the card and ends the soft-ask, verbatim in both places. 'Profile → Notifications → Off' quotes this app's own UI path.

Notifications.card.notConfigured.source: Push notifications aren’t set up for this deployment yet — an admin can enable them in Admin → Settings.
Notifications.card.notConfigured.context: Muted note when the deployment has no push configured; points admins to Admin → Settings (quotes app UI names).

Notifications.card.loadFailed.source: Could not load your notification settings.
Notifications.card.loadFailed.context: Error line when the settings fetch fails.

Notifications.card.saveFailed.context: Error line when saving a toggle fails.

Notifications.card.enable.context: Primary button starting the enable flow (opens the soft-ask dialog). Short.

Notifications.card.enableFailed.source: Could not turn notifications on — please try again.
Notifications.card.enableFailed.context: Error line when token registration fails after permission was granted.

Notifications.card.thisDeviceOn.context: Status line: the device you are looking at is registered. Pairs with the 'Turn off on this device' button.

Notifications.card.thisDeviceOff.context: Button: stop notifications on the current device only (account stays on). Short.

Notifications.card.thisDeviceEnable.context: Button: account is on but THIS device isn't registered yet. Short.

Notifications.card.noDevices.source: Notifications are on, but no device is currently receiving them.
Notifications.card.noDevices.context: Amber banner: account-level on but zero registered devices — nothing will actually arrive. Also shown app-wide.

Notifications.card.devices.context: List heading for registered devices. Short.

Notifications.card.deviceCurrent.context: Tiny badge marking the device row you are on. One-two words.

Notifications.card.deviceUnnamed.context: Fallback device label when no browser label was captured. Short.

Notifications.card.deviceRemove.context: Button on a device row: deregister that device. One word.

Notifications.card.deviceRemoveAria.context: aria-label of the remove button; {label} is the device label ('Safari · iPhone'), verbatim.

Notifications.card.selfTest.context: Button: send yourself one test notification (the 'is it working?' check). Short.

Notifications.card.selfTestSent.source: Sent at {time} — did this device show it?
Notifications.card.selfTestSent.context: In-page confirmation after the test send; {time} is a localized clock time. The question makes the user verify the phone actually showed it.

Notifications.card.accountOff.context: The master OFF button (danger style): stops all delivery server-side for the whole account. Short.

Notifications.card.turnOn.context: Generic toggle button label used on category rows. One-two words. Pairs with turnOff.

Notifications.card.turnOff.context: Generic toggle button label used on category rows. One-two words. Pairs with turnOn.

Notifications.categories.title.context: Heading above the per-category toggle rows. Short.

Notifications.categories.signing.name.context: Category toggle name: pushes about claims awaiting YOUR signature (shown to approvers only). Short.

Notifications.categories.signing.example.context: Example line under the toggle AND a bullet in the soft-ask; quotes the actual push title format with a sample label.

Notifications.categories.claims.name.context: Category toggle name: pushes about the user's OWN claims. Short.

Notifications.categories.claims.example.context: Example line; quotes the approved-push title.

Notifications.categories.finance.name.context: Category toggle name for treasurers: approved claims awaiting payment. Deliberately plain-language ('Payments to make'), NOT the jargon 'Finance'. Short.

Notifications.categories.finance.example.source: e.g. “A claim is ready for payment”
Notifications.categories.finance.example.context: Example line; quotes the finance push title.

Notifications.categories.security.name.context: Category toggle name: new-device security alerts. One word.

Notifications.categories.security.exampl.source: e.g. “A new device asked to join your signing identity”
Notifications.categories.security.exampl.context: Example line; quotes the device-request push title.

Notifications.categories.discreet.name.context: Toggle name for discreet lock-screen previews. Short.

Notifications.categories.discreet.exampl.source: Lock-screen text hides names and outcomes — “An update on one of your claims”.
Notifications.categories.discreet.exampl.context: Explains what discreet mode does and quotes the neutral preview text.

Notifications.softAsk.title.context: Dialog heading of the pre-permission explainer ('soft ask'). Question form.

Notifications.softAsk.body.source: Numbers will notify this device about:
Notifications.softAsk.body.context: Line above the bullet list of what the user will be notified about. 'Numbers' verbatim.

Notifications.softAsk.privacy.source: Only you can see your notification settings. No one is told whether a notification reached you, or when.
Notifications.softAsk.privacy.context: Privacy promise in the soft-ask — key trust sentence, translate precisely: nobody can see settings or delivery state.

Notifications.softAsk.noDuty.source: This doesn’t create a duty to respond faster — the badges in the app remain the official queue.
Notifications.softAsk.noDuty.context: Reassurance for role-holders: enabling creates no obligation to answer faster. 'badges' = the red count dots in this app's navigation.

Notifications.softAsk.reassign.source: Owners can withdraw or reassign a signature request at any time — that is routine, not a judgment.
Notifications.softAsk.reassign.context: Normalization line for approvers: vanished requests are routine workflow (withdrawal/reassignment), NEVER censure. Must not imply cause or blame.

Notifications.softAsk.shared.source: Is this a shared computer or a family tablet? Don’t turn notifications on here — or use discreet previews.
Notifications.softAsk.shared.context: Warning line about shared computers / family tablets, pointing at discreet previews as the alternative.

Notifications.softAsk.promptHint.source: Next, your device asks permission — choose “Allow”.
Notifications.softAsk.promptHint.context: Line right before the native browser permission dialog; “Allow” quotes the OS button and must NOT be naturalized — keep the English word, optionally adding the Chinese beside it.

Notifications.softAsk.promptHintOs.source: Your device may show that dialog in its own language; “Allow / 允許” is the agree button.
Notifications.softAsk.promptHintOs.context: Shown when the device language differs from the app language. “Allow / 允許” quotes OS chrome verbatim in both languages — do not alter.

Notifications.softAsk.confirm.context: Soft-ask confirm button (then the native prompt fires). One word.

Notifications.softAsk.decline.context: Soft-ask decline button — genuinely neutral, no guilt. Short.

Notifications.embedded.title.context: Heading when the app runs inside WeChat/Line/etc. where notifications are impossible. Short.

Notifications.embedded.body.source: Notifications can’t be turned on inside this app (WeChat, Line…). Open Numbers in Safari or Chrome, then come back to this page.
Notifications.embedded.body.context: Explains to open the site in a real browser; 'WeChat, Line' are product names, keep recognizable (微信 / LINE fine).

Notifications.embedded.copyLink.context: Button copying the app URL. Short.

Notifications.embedded.copied.context: Button label after copying. One word.

Notifications.iosInstall.title.source: Add Numbers to your Home Screen first
Notifications.iosInstall.title.context: Heading of the iPhone install instructions. 'Home Screen' quotes iOS chrome — dual-language per glossary if needed.

Notifications.iosInstall.step1.source: 1. In Safari, tap the Share button (the square with an arrow).
Notifications.iosInstall.step1.context: iOS install step; 'Share' quotes the iOS button name — do not naturalize away the English.

Notifications.iosInstall.step2.context: iOS install step; “Add to Home Screen” quotes the iOS menu item verbatim — keep the English, Chinese may follow in parentheses.

Notifications.iosInstall.step3.source: 3. Open the new Numbers icon, sign in there, and turn notifications on.
Notifications.iosInstall.step3.context: iOS install step: open the installed icon, sign in, enable.

Notifications.iosInstall.note.source: The installed app counts as a new device for signing — if you e-sign, approve it from here when it asks. If you stop partway, the app will remind you to finish; nothing is lost.
Notifications.iosInstall.note.context: Reassurance: the installed app is a NEW signing device (typed-code approval will run), and abandoning mid-flow is safe/resumable.

Notifications.iosInstall.started.context: Button the user taps after adding the icon. Short.

Notifications.iosInstall.marked.source: Great — continue in the installed app.
Notifications.iosInstall.marked.context: Confirmation after tapping 'I've added it' — continue inside the installed app.

Notifications.iosOld.body.source: This iPhone’s software is too old for web notifications — iOS 16.4 or later is required.
Notifications.iosOld.body.context: Honest dead-end for iOS 15 and older; no workaround exists.

Notifications.unsupported.body.source: This browser can’t receive push notifications.
Notifications.unsupported.body.context: Honest dead-end for browsers without the Push API.

Notifications.denied.title.source: Notifications are blocked by this browser
Notifications.denied.title.context: Heading when browser permission is permanently denied. Short.

Notifications.denied.desktop.source: Click the lock icon in the address bar → “Site settings” → allow Notifications, then try again.
Notifications.denied.desktop.context: Desktop/Android unblock path; 'Site settings' quotes browser chrome — keep English, may add Chinese beside.

Notifications.denied.ios.source: Remove the app icon from your Home Screen and add it again (Share → “Add to Home Screen”), then choose Allow when asked. Removing the icon also removes this device’s signing key — you’ll approve this device again afterwards.
Notifications.denied.ios.context: iOS recovery: remove + re-add the Home Screen icon (the honest path — there is no settings toggle before first grant). Warns the signing key resets. “Add to Home Screen” quotes iOS chrome.

Notifications.runtime.reconnect.context: Chip button re-registering this device. One word.

Notifications.runtime.reconnectBody.source: Notifications are on for your account, but this device stopped receiving them.
Notifications.runtime.reconnectBody.context: Banner text when the account is on but THIS device silently stopped receiving (phone migration, storage eviction).

Notifications.runtime.resumeBody.source: Finish setting up notifications — you’re almost there.
Notifications.runtime.resumeBody.context: Banner text resuming the abandoned iPhone setup. Encouraging, short.

Notifications.runtime.openSettings.context: Banner button to the profile notifications card. Short.

Notifications.runtime.dismiss.context: Dismiss button (also aria-label of the ✕). One word.

Notifications.runtime.open.context: Toast button opening the notification's target page. One word.

Notifications.runtime.openWhenReady.source: A notification arrived — open it when you’re ready (your receipts are still processing).
Notifications.runtime.openWhenReady.context: Toast shown when a notification arrives while receipts are mid-extraction: we refuse to navigate away; parenthetical explains why.

Notifications.activity.title.context: Home-page card heading for the recent-activity list. Short.

Notifications.activity.deletedClaim.context: Substituted for the claim label when the claim was deleted; lowercase, slots into titles like 'Signature requested — a deleted claim'.

Notifications.activity.byName.context: Small attribution line under an activity row; {name} is a member name, verbatim. Lowercase 'by'.
